17/*
18 * DAV extension module for Apache 2.0.*
19 *
20 * This module is repository-independent. It depends on hooks provided by a
21 * repository implementation.
22 *
23 * APACHE ISSUES:
24 *   - within a DAV hierarchy, if an unknown method is used and we default
25 *     to Apache's implementation, it sends back an OPTIONS with the wrong
26 *     set of methods -- there is NO HOOK for us.
27 *     therefore: we need to manually handle the HTTP_METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED
28 *       and HTTP_NOT_IMPLEMENTED responses (not ap_send_error_response).
29 *   - process_mkcol_body() had to dup code from ap_setup_client_block().
30 *   - it would be nice to get status lines from Apache for arbitrary
31 *     status codes
32 *   - it would be nice to be able to extend Apache's set of response
33 *     codes so that it doesn't return 500 when an unknown code is placed
34 *     into r->status.
35 *   - http_vhost functions should apply "const" to their params
36 *
37 * DESIGN NOTES:
38 *   - For PROPFIND, we batch up the entire response in memory before
39 *     sending it. We may want to reorganize around sending the information
40 *     as we suck it in from the propdb. Alternatively, we should at least
41 *     generate a total Content-Length if we're going to buffer in memory
42 *     so that we can keep the connection open.
43 */ view japanese
44
45#include "apr_strings.h"
46#include "apr_lib.h"            /* for apr_is* */ view japanese

65/* ### what is the best way to set this? */ view japanese
66#define DAV_DEFAULT_PROVIDER    "filesystem"
67
68/* used to denote that mod_dav will be handling this request */ view japanese
69#define DAV_HANDLER_NAME "dav-handler"
70
71APLOG_USE_MODULE(dav);
72
73enum {
74    DAV_ENABLED_UNSET = 0,
75    DAV_ENABLED_OFF,
76    DAV_ENABLED_ON
77};
78
79/* per-dir configuration */ view japanese
80typedef struct {
81    const char *provider_name;
82    const dav_provider *provider;
83    const char *dir;
84    const char *base;
85    int locktimeout;
86    int allow_depthinfinity;
87    int allow_lockdiscovery;
88
89} dav_dir_conf;
90
91/* per-server configuration */ view japanese
92typedef struct {
93    int unused;
94
95} dav_server_conf;
96
97#define DAV_INHERIT_VALUE(parent, child, field) \
98                ((child)->field ? (child)->field : (parent)->field)
99
100
101/* forward-declare for use in configuration lookup */ view japanese
102extern module DAV_DECLARE_DATA dav_module;
103
104/* DAV methods */ view japanese
105enum {
106    DAV_M_BIND = 0,
107    DAV_M_SEARCH,
108    DAV_M_LAST
109};
110static int dav_methods[DAV_M_LAST];
111
112
113static int dav_init_handler(apr_pool_t *p, apr_pool_t *plog, apr_pool_t *ptemp,
114                             server_rec *s)
115{
116    /* DBG0("dav_init_handler"); */ view japanese
117
118    /* Register DAV methods */ view japanese
119    dav_methods[DAV_M_BIND] = ap_method_register(p, "BIND");
120    dav_methods[DAV_M_SEARCH] = ap_method_register(p, "SEARCH");
121
122    return OK;
123}
124
125static void *dav_create_server_config(apr_pool_t *p, server_rec *s)
126{
127    dav_server_conf *newconf;
128
129    newconf = (dav_server_conf *)apr_pcalloc(p, sizeof(*newconf));
130
131    /* ### this isn't used at the moment... */ view japanese
132
133    return newconf;
134}
135
136static void *dav_merge_server_config(apr_pool_t *p, void *base, void *overrides)
137{
138#if 0
139    dav_server_conf *child = overrides;
140#endif
141    dav_server_conf *newconf;
142
143    newconf = (dav_server_conf *)apr_pcalloc(p, sizeof(*newconf));
144
145    /* ### nothing to merge right now... */ view japanese
146
147    return newconf;
148}
149
150static void *dav_create_dir_config(apr_pool_t *p, char *dir)
151{
152    /* NOTE: dir==NULL creates the default per-dir config */ view japanese
153
154    dav_dir_conf *conf;
155
156    conf = (dav_dir_conf *)apr_pcalloc(p, sizeof(*conf));
157
158    /* clean up the directory to remove any trailing slash */ view japanese
159    if (dir != NULL) {
160        char *d;
161        apr_size_t l;
162
163        l = strlen(dir);
164        d = apr_pstrmemdup(p, dir, l);
165        if (l > 1 && d[l - 1] == '/')
166            d[l - 1] = '\0';
167        conf->dir = d;
168    }
169
170    return conf;
171}
172
173static void *dav_merge_dir_config(apr_pool_t *p, void *base, void *overrides)
174{
175    dav_dir_conf *parent = base;
176    dav_dir_conf *child = overrides;
177    dav_dir_conf *newconf = (dav_dir_conf *)apr_pcalloc(p, sizeof(*newconf));
178
179    /* DBG3("dav_merge_dir_config: new=%08lx  base=%08lx  overrides=%08lx",
180       (long)newconf, (long)base, (long)overrides); */ view japanese
181
182    newconf->provider_name = DAV_INHERIT_VALUE(parent, child, provider_name);
183    newconf->provider = DAV_INHERIT_VALUE(parent, child, provider);
184    if (parent->provider_name != NULL) {
185        if (child->provider_name == NULL) {
186            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, NULL, APLOGNO(00578)
187                         "\"DAV Off\" cannot be used to turn off a subtree "
188                         "of a DAV-enabled location.");
189        }
190        else if (strcasecmp(child->provider_name,
191                            parent->provider_name) != 0) {
192            ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, NULL, APLOGNO(00579)
193                         "A subtree cannot specify a different DAV provider "
194                         "than its parent.");
195        }
196    }
197
198    newconf->locktimeout = DAV_INHERIT_VALUE(parent, child, locktimeout);
199    newconf->dir = DAV_INHERIT_VALUE(parent, child, dir);
200    newconf->base = DAV_INHERIT_VALUE(parent, child, base);
201    newconf->allow_depthinfinity = DAV_INHERIT_VALUE(parent, child,
202                                                     allow_depthinfinity);
203    newconf->allow_lockdiscovery = DAV_INHERIT_VALUE(parent, child,
204                                                     allow_lockdiscovery);
205
206    return newconf;
207}
208
209DAV_DECLARE(const char *) dav_get_provider_name(request_rec *r)
210{
211    dav_dir_conf *conf = ap_get_module_config(r->per_dir_config, &dav_module);
212    return conf ? conf->provider_name : NULL;
213}
214
215DAV_DECLARE(const dav_provider *) dav_get_provider(request_rec *r)
216{
217    dav_dir_conf *conf;
218
219    conf = ap_get_module_config(r->per_dir_config, &dav_module);
220    /* assert: conf->provider_name != NULL
221       (otherwise, DAV is disabled, and we wouldn't be here) */ view japanese
222
223    /* assert: conf->provider != NULL
224       (checked when conf->provider_name is set) */ view japanese
225    return conf->provider;
226}
227
228DAV_DECLARE(const dav_hooks_locks *) dav_get_lock_hooks(request_rec *r)
229{
230    return dav_get_provider(r)->locks;
231}
232
233DAV_DECLARE(const dav_hooks_propdb *) dav_get_propdb_hooks(request_rec *r)
234{
235    return dav_get_provider(r)->propdb;
236}
237
238DAV_DECLARE(const dav_hooks_vsn *) dav_get_vsn_hooks(request_rec *r)
239{
240    return dav_get_provider(r)->vsn;
241}
242
243DAV_DECLARE(const dav_hooks_binding *) dav_get_binding_hooks(request_rec *r)
244{
245    return dav_get_provider(r)->binding;
246}
247
248DAV_DECLARE(const dav_hooks_search *) dav_get_search_hooks(request_rec *r)
249{
250    return dav_get_provider(r)->search;
251}
252
253DAV_DECLARE(const char *) dav_get_base_path(request_rec *r)
254{
255    dav_dir_conf *conf = ap_get_module_config(r->per_dir_config, &dav_module);
256
257    return conf && conf->base ? conf->base : NULL;
258}
259
260/*
261 * Command handler for the DAV directive, which is TAKE1.
262 */ view japanese
263static const char *dav_cmd_dav(cmd_parms *cmd, void *config, const char *arg1)
264{
265    dav_dir_conf *conf = (dav_dir_conf *)config;
266
267    if (strcasecmp(arg1, "on") == 0) {
268        conf->provider_name = DAV_DEFAULT_PROVIDER;
269    }
270    else if (strcasecmp(arg1, "off") == 0) {
271        conf->provider_name = NULL;
272        conf->provider = NULL;
273    }
274    else {
275        conf->provider_name = arg1;
276    }
277
278    if (conf->provider_name != NULL) {
279        /* lookup and cache the actual provider now */ view japanese
280        conf->provider = dav_lookup_provider(conf->provider_name);
281
282        if (conf->provider == NULL) {
283            /* by the time they use it, the provider should be loaded and
284               registered with us. */ view japanese
285            return apr_psprintf(cmd->pool,
286                                "Unknown DAV provider: %s",
287                                conf->provider_name);
288        }
289    }
290
291    return NULL;
292}
293
294/*
295 * Command handler for the DAVBasePath directive, which is TAKE1
296 */ view japanese
297static const char *dav_cmd_davbasepath(cmd_parms *cmd, void *config, const char *arg1)
298{
299    dav_dir_conf *conf = config;
300
301    conf->base = arg1;
302
303    return NULL;
304}
305
306/*
307 * Command handler for the DAVDepthInfinity directive, which is FLAG.
308 */ view japanese
309static const char *dav_cmd_davdepthinfinity(cmd_parms *cmd, void *config,
310                                            int arg)
311{
312    dav_dir_conf *conf = (dav_dir_conf *)config;
313
314    if (arg)
315        conf->allow_depthinfinity = DAV_ENABLED_ON;
316    else
317        conf->allow_depthinfinity = DAV_ENABLED_OFF;
318    return NULL;
319}
320
321/*
322 * Command handler for the DAVLockDiscovery directive, which is FLAG.
323 */ view japanese
324static const char *dav_cmd_davlockdiscovery(cmd_parms *cmd, void *config,
325                                            int arg)
326{
327    dav_dir_conf *conf = (dav_dir_conf *)config;
328
329    if (arg)
330        conf->allow_lockdiscovery = DAV_ENABLED_ON;
331    else
332        conf->allow_lockdiscovery = DAV_ENABLED_OFF;
333    return NULL;
334}
335
336/*
337 * Command handler for DAVMinTimeout directive, which is TAKE1
338 */ view japanese
339static const char *dav_cmd_davmintimeout(cmd_parms *cmd, void *config,
340                                         const char *arg1)
341{
342    dav_dir_conf *conf = (dav_dir_conf *)config;
343
344    conf->locktimeout = atoi(arg1);
345    if (conf->locktimeout < 0)
346        return "DAVMinTimeout requires a non-negative integer.";
347
348    return NULL;
349}
350
351/*
352** dav_error_response()
353**
354** Send a nice response back to the user. In most cases, Apache doesn't
355** allow us to provide details in the body about what happened. This
356** function allows us to completely specify the response body.
357**
358** ### this function is not logging any errors! (e.g. the body)
359*/ view japanese
360static int dav_error_response(request_rec *r, int status, const char *body)
361{
362    r->status = status;
363    r->status_line = ap_get_status_line(status);
364
365    ap_set_content_type_ex(r, "text/html; charset=ISO-8859-1", 1);
366
367    /* begin the response now... */ view japanese
368    ap_rvputs(r,
369              DAV_RESPONSE_BODY_1,
370              r->status_line,
371              DAV_RESPONSE_BODY_2,
372              &r->status_line[4],
373              DAV_RESPONSE_BODY_3,
374              body,
375              DAV_RESPONSE_BODY_4,
376              ap_psignature("<hr />\n", r),
377              DAV_RESPONSE_BODY_5,
378              NULL);
379
380    /* the response has been sent. */
381    /*
382     * ### Use of DONE obviates logging..!
383     */
384    return DONE;
385}
386
387
388/*
389 * Send a "standardized" error response based on the error's namespace & tag
390 */ view japanese
391static int dav_error_response_tag(request_rec *r,
392                                  dav_error *err)
393{
394    r->status = err->status;
395
396    ap_set_content_type_ex(r, DAV_XML_CONTENT_TYPE, 1);
397
398    ap_rputs(DAV_XML_HEADER DEBUG_CR
399             "<D:error xmlns:D=\"DAV:\"", r);
400
401    if (err->desc != NULL) {
402        /* ### should move this namespace somewhere (with the others!) */ view japanese
403        ap_rputs(" xmlns:m=\"http://apache.org/dav/xmlns\"", r); view japanese
404    }
405
406    if (err->childtags) {
407        if (err->namespace != NULL) {
408            ap_rprintf(r,
409                    " xmlns:C=\"%s\">" DEBUG_CR
410                    "<C:%s>%s</C:%s>" DEBUG_CR,
411                    err->namespace,
412                    err->tagname, err->childtags, err->tagname);
413        }
414        else {
415            ap_rprintf(r,
416                    ">" DEBUG_CR
417                    "<D:%s>%s</D:%s>" DEBUG_CR,
418                    err->tagname, err->childtags, err->tagname);
419        }
420    }
421    else {
422        if (err->namespace != NULL) {
423            ap_rprintf(r,
424                    " xmlns:C=\"%s\">" DEBUG_CR
425                    "<C:%s/>" DEBUG_CR,
426                    err->namespace, err->tagname);
427        }
428        else {
429            ap_rprintf(r,
430                    ">" DEBUG_CR
431                    "<D:%s/>" DEBUG_CR, err->tagname);
432        }
433    }
434
435    /* here's our mod_dav specific tag: */ view japanese
436    if (err->desc != NULL) {
437        ap_rprintf(r,
438                   "<m:human-readable errcode=\"%d\">" DEBUG_CR
439                   "%s" DEBUG_CR
440                   "</m:human-readable>" DEBUG_CR,
441                   err->error_id,
442                   apr_xml_quote_string(r->pool, err->desc, 0));
443    }
444
445    ap_rputs("</D:error>" DEBUG_CR, r);
446
447    /* the response has been sent. */
448    /*
449     * ### Use of DONE obviates logging..!
450     */ view japanese
451    return DONE;
452}
453
454
455/*
456 * Apache's URI escaping does not replace '&' since that is a valid character
457 * in a URI (to form a query section). We must explicitly handle it so that
458 * we can embed the URI into an XML document.
459 */ view japanese
460static const char *dav_xml_escape_uri(apr_pool_t *p, const char *uri)
461{
462    const char *e_uri = ap_escape_uri(p, uri);
463
464    /* check the easy case... */ view japanese
465    if (ap_strchr_c(e_uri, '&') == NULL)
466        return e_uri;
467
468    /* there was a '&', so more work is needed... sigh. */ view japanese
469
470    /*
471     * Note: this is a teeny bit of overkill since we know there are no
472     * '<' or '>' characters, but who cares.
473     */ view japanese
474    return apr_xml_quote_string(p, e_uri, 0);
475}
476
477
478/* Write a complete RESPONSE object out as a <DAV:response> xml
479   element.  Data is sent into brigade BB, which is auto-flushed into
480   the output filter stack for request R.  Use POOL for any temporary
481   allocations.
482
483   [Presumably the <multistatus> tag has already been written;  this
484   routine is shared by dav_send_multistatus and dav_stream_response.]
485*/ view japanese
486DAV_DECLARE(void) dav_send_one_response(dav_response *response,
487                                        apr_bucket_brigade *bb,
488                                        request_rec *r,
489                                        apr_pool_t *pool)
490{
491    apr_text *t = NULL;
492
493    if (response->propresult.xmlns == NULL) {
494      ap_fputs(r->output_filters, bb, "<D:response>");
495    }
496    else {
497      ap_fputs(r->output_filters, bb, "<D:response");
498      for (t = response->propresult.xmlns; t; t = t->next) {
499        ap_fputs(r->output_filters, bb, t->text);
500      }
501      ap_fputc(r->output_filters, bb, '>');
502    }
503
504    ap_fputstrs(r->output_filters, bb,
505                DEBUG_CR "<D:href>",
506                dav_xml_escape_uri(pool, response->href),
507                "</D:href>" DEBUG_CR,
508                NULL);
509
510    if (response->propresult.propstats == NULL) {
511      /* use the Status-Line text from Apache.  Note, this will
512       * default to 500 Internal Server Error if first->status
513       * is not a known (or valid) status code.
514       */ view japanese
515      ap_fputstrs(r->output_filters, bb,
516                  "<D:status>HTTP/1.1 ",
517                  ap_get_status_line(response->status),
518                  "</D:status>" DEBUG_CR,
519                  NULL);
520    }
521    else {
522      /* assume this includes <propstat> and is quoted properly */ view japanese
523      for (t = response->propresult.propstats; t; t = t->next) {
524        ap_fputs(r->output_filters, bb, t->text);
525      }
526    }
527
528    if (response->desc != NULL) {
529      /*
530       * We supply the description, so we know it doesn't have to
531       * have any escaping/encoding applied to it.
532       */ view japanese
533      ap_fputstrs(r->output_filters, bb,
534                  "<D:responsedescription>",
535                  response->desc,
536                  "</D:responsedescription>" DEBUG_CR,
537                  NULL);
538    }
539
540    ap_fputs(r->output_filters, bb, "</D:response>" DEBUG_CR);
541}
542
543
544/* Factorized helper function: prep request_rec R for a multistatus
545   response and write <multistatus> tag into BB, destined for
546   R->output_filters.  Use xml NAMESPACES in initial tag, if
547   non-NULL. */ view japanese
548DAV_DECLARE(void) dav_begin_multistatus(apr_bucket_brigade *bb,
549                                        request_rec *r, int status,
550                                        apr_array_header_t *namespaces)
551{
552    /* Set the correct status and Content-Type */ view japanese
553    r->status = status;
554    ap_set_content_type_ex(r, DAV_XML_CONTENT_TYPE, 1);
555
556    /* Send the headers and actual multistatus response now... */ view japanese
557    ap_fputs(r->output_filters, bb, DAV_XML_HEADER DEBUG_CR
558             "<D:multistatus xmlns:D=\"DAV:\"");
559
560    if (namespaces != NULL) {
561       int i;
562
563       for (i = namespaces->nelts; i--; ) {
564           ap_fprintf(r->output_filters, bb, " xmlns:ns%d=\"%s\"", i,
565                      APR_XML_GET_URI_ITEM(namespaces, i));
566       }
567    }
568
569    ap_fputs(r->output_filters, bb, ">" DEBUG_CR);
570}
571
572/* Finish a multistatus response started by dav_begin_multistatus: */ view japanese
573DAV_DECLARE(apr_status_t) dav_finish_multistatus(request_rec *r,
574                                                 apr_bucket_brigade *bb)
575{
576    apr_bucket *b;
577
578    ap_fputs(r->output_filters, bb, "</D:multistatus>" DEBUG_CR);
579
580    /* indicate the end of the response body */ view japanese
581    b = apr_bucket_eos_create(r->connection->bucket_alloc);
582    APR_BRIGADE_INSERT_TAIL(bb, b);
583
584    /* deliver whatever might be remaining in the brigade */ view japanese
585    return ap_pass_brigade(r->output_filters, bb);
586}
587
588DAV_DECLARE(void) dav_send_multistatus(request_rec *r, int status,
589                                       dav_response *first,
590                                       apr_array_header_t *namespaces)
591{
592    apr_pool_t *subpool;
593    apr_bucket_brigade *bb = apr_brigade_create(r->pool,
594                                                r->connection->bucket_alloc);
595
596    dav_begin_multistatus(bb, r, status, namespaces);
597
598    apr_pool_create(&subpool, r->pool);
599    apr_pool_tag(subpool, "mod_dav-multistatus");
600
601    for (; first != NULL; first = first->next) {
602      apr_pool_clear(subpool);
603      dav_send_one_response(first, bb, r, subpool);
604    }
605    apr_pool_destroy(subpool);
606
607    dav_finish_multistatus(r, bb);
608}
609
610/*
611 * dav_log_err()
612 *
613 * Write error information to the log.
614 */ view japanese
615static void dav_log_err(request_rec *r, dav_error *err, int level)
616{
617    dav_error *errscan;
618
619    /* Log the errors */ view japanese
620    /* ### should have a directive to log the first or all */ view japanese
621    for (errscan = err; errscan != NULL; errscan = errscan->prev) {
622        if (errscan->desc == NULL)
623            continue;
624
625        /* Intentional no APLOGNO */ view japanese
626        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, level, errscan->aprerr, r, "%s  [%d, #%d]",
627                      errscan->desc, errscan->status, errscan->error_id);
628    }
629}
630
631/*
632 * dav_handle_err()
633 *
634 * Handle the standard error processing. <err> must be non-NULL.
635 *
636 * <response> is set by the following:
637 *   - dav_validate_request()
638 *   - dav_add_lock()
639 *   - repos_hooks->remove_resource
640 *   - repos_hooks->move_resource
641 *   - repos_hooks->copy_resource
642 *   - vsn_hooks->update
643 */ view japanese
644DAV_DECLARE(int) dav_handle_err(request_rec *r, dav_error *err,
645                                dav_response *response)
646{
647    /* log the errors */ view japanese
648    dav_log_err(r, err, APLOG_ERR);
649
650    if (!ap_is_HTTP_VALID_RESPONSE(err->status)) {
651        /* we have responded already */ view japanese
652        return AP_FILTER_ERROR;
653    }
654
655    if (response == NULL) {
656        dav_error *stackerr = err;
657
658        /* our error messages are safe; tell Apache this */ view japanese
659        apr_table_setn(r->notes, "verbose-error-to", "*");
660
661        /* Didn't get a multistatus response passed in, but we still
662           might be able to generate a standard <D:error> response.
663           Search the error stack for an errortag. */ view japanese
664        while (stackerr != NULL && stackerr->tagname == NULL)
665            stackerr = stackerr->prev;
666
667        if (stackerr != NULL && stackerr->tagname != NULL)
668            return dav_error_response_tag(r, stackerr);
669
670        return err->status;
671    }
672
673    /* send the multistatus and tell Apache the request/response is DONE. */ view japanese
674    dav_send_multistatus(r, err->status, response, NULL);
675    return DONE;
676}
677
678/* handy function for return values of methods that (may) create things.
679 * locn if provided is assumed to be escaped. */ view japanese
680static int dav_created(request_rec *r, const char *locn, const char *what,
681                       int replaced)
682{
683    const char *body;
684
685    if (locn == NULL) {
686        locn = ap_escape_uri(r->pool, r->uri);
687    }
688
689    /* did the target resource already exist? */ view japanese
690    if (replaced) {
691        /* Apache will supply a default message */ view japanese
692        return HTTP_NO_CONTENT;
693    }
694
695    /* Per HTTP/1.1, S10.2.2: add a Location header to contain the
696     * URI that was created. */ view japanese
697
698    /* Convert locn to an absolute URI, and return in Location header */ view japanese
699    apr_table_setn(r->headers_out, "Location", ap_construct_url(r->pool, locn, r));
700
701    /* ### insert an ETag header? see HTTP/1.1 S10.2.2 */ view japanese
702
703    /* Apache doesn't allow us to set a variable body for HTTP_CREATED, so
704     * we must manufacture the entire response. */ view japanese
705    body = apr_pstrcat(r->pool, what, " ", ap_escape_html(r->pool, locn),
706                       " has been created.", NULL);
707    return dav_error_response(r, HTTP_CREATED, body);
708}
709
710/* ### move to dav_util? */ view japanese
711DAV_DECLARE(int) dav_get_depth(request_rec *r, int def_depth)
712{
713    const char *depth = apr_table_get(r->headers_in, "Depth");
714
715    if (depth == NULL) {
716        return def_depth;
717    }
718
719    if (ap_cstr_casecmp(depth, "infinity") == 0) {
720        return DAV_INFINITY;
721    }
722    else if (strcmp(depth, "0") == 0) {
723        return 0;
724    }
725    else if (strcmp(depth, "1") == 0) {
726        return 1;
727    }
728
729    /* The caller will return an HTTP_BAD_REQUEST. This will augment the
730     * default message that Apache provides. */
731    ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00580)
732                  "An invalid Depth header was specified.");
733    return -1;
734}
735
736static int dav_get_overwrite(request_rec *r)
737{
738    const char *overwrite = apr_table_get(r->headers_in, "Overwrite");
739
740    if (overwrite == NULL) {
741        return 1; /* default is "T" */ view japanese
742    }
743
744    if ((*overwrite == 'F' || *overwrite == 'f') && overwrite[1] == '\0') {
745        return 0;
746    }
747
748    if ((*overwrite == 'T' || *overwrite == 't') && overwrite[1] == '\0') {
749        return 1;
750    }
751
752    /* The caller will return an HTTP_BAD_REQUEST. This will augment the
753     * default message that Apache provides. */ view japanese
754    ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00581)
755                  "An invalid Overwrite header was specified.");
756    return -1;
757}
758
759/* resolve a request URI to a resource descriptor.
760 *
761 * If label_allowed != 0, then allow the request target to be altered by
762 * a Label: header.
763 *
764 * If use_checked_in is true, then the repository provider should return
765 * the resource identified by the DAV:checked-in property of the resource
766 * identified by the Request-URI.
767 */ view japanese
768DAV_DECLARE(dav_error *) dav_get_resource(request_rec *r, int label_allowed,
769                                   int use_checked_in, dav_resource **res_p)
770{
771    dav_dir_conf *conf;
772    const char *label = NULL, *base;
773    dav_error *err;
774
775    /* if the request target can be overridden, get any target selector */ view japanese
776    if (label_allowed) {
777        label = apr_table_get(r->headers_in, "label");
778    }
779
780    conf = ap_get_module_config(r->per_dir_config, &dav_module);
781    /* assert: conf->provider != NULL */ view japanese
782    if (conf->provider == NULL) {
783        return dav_new_error(r->pool, HTTP_METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED, 0, 0,
784                             apr_psprintf(r->pool,
785                             "DAV not enabled for %s",
786                             ap_escape_html(r->pool, r->uri)));
787    }
788
789    /* Take the repos root from DAVBasePath if configured, else the
790     * path of the enclosing section. */ view japanese
791    base = conf->base ? conf->base : conf->dir;
792
793    /* resolve the resource */
794    err = (*conf->provider->repos->get_resource)(r, base,
795                                                 label, use_checked_in,
796                                                 res_p);
797    if (err != NULL) {
798        /* In the error path, give a hint that DavBasePath needs to be
799         * used if the location was configured via a regex match. */ view japanese
800        if (!conf->base) {
801            core_dir_config *cdc = ap_get_core_module_config(r->per_dir_config);
802
803            if (cdc->r) {
804                ap_log_error(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, NULL, APLOGNO(10484)
805                             "failed to find repository for location configured "
806                             "via regex match - missing DAVBasePath?");
807            }
808        }
809
810        err = dav_push_error(r->pool, err->status, 0,
811                             "Could not fetch resource information.", err);
812        return err;
813    }
814
815    /* Note: this shouldn't happen, but just be sure... */ view japanese
816    if (*res_p == NULL) {
817        /* ### maybe use HTTP_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR */ view japanese
818        return dav_new_error(r->pool, HTTP_NOT_FOUND, 0, 0,
819                             apr_psprintf(r->pool,
820                                          "The provider did not define a "
821                                          "resource for %s.",
822                                          ap_escape_html(r->pool, r->uri)));
823    }
824
825    /* ### hmm. this doesn't feel like the right place or thing to do */ view japanese
826    /* if there were any input headers requiring a Vary header in the response,
827     * add it now */ view japanese
828    dav_add_vary_header(r, r, *res_p);
829
830    return NULL;
831}
832
833DAV_DECLARE(dav_error *) dav_open_lockdb(request_rec *r,
834                                         int ro,
835                                         dav_lockdb **lockdb)
836{
837    const dav_hooks_locks *hooks = DAV_GET_HOOKS_LOCKS(r);
838
839    if (hooks == NULL) {
840        *lockdb = NULL;
841        return NULL;
842    }
843
844    /* open the thing lazily */ view japanese
845    return (*hooks->open_lockdb)(r, ro, 0, lockdb);
846}
847
848DAV_DECLARE(void) dav_close_lockdb(dav_lockdb *lockdb)
849{
850    (lockdb->hooks->close_lockdb)(lockdb);
851}
852
853/**
854 * @return  1 if valid content-range,
855 *          0 if no content-range,
856 *         -1 if malformed content-range
857 */ view japanese
858static int dav_parse_range(request_rec *r,
859                           apr_off_t *range_start, apr_off_t *range_end)
860{
861    const char *range_c;
862    char *range;
863    char *dash;
864    char *slash;
865
866    range_c = apr_table_get(r->headers_in, "content-range");
867    if (range_c == NULL)
868        return 0;
869
870    range = apr_pstrdup(r->pool, range_c);
871    if (ap_cstr_casecmpn(range, "bytes ", 6) != 0
872        || (dash = ap_strchr(range + 6, '-')) == NULL
873        || (slash = ap_strchr(range + 6, '/')) == NULL) {
874        /* malformed header */ view japanese
875        return -1;
876    }
877
878    *dash++ = *slash++ = '\0';
879
880    /* detect invalid ranges */ view japanese
881    if (!ap_parse_strict_length(range_start, range + 6)) {
882        return -1;
883    }
884    if (!ap_parse_strict_length(range_end, dash)
885            || *range_end < *range_start) {
886        return -1;
887    }
888
889    if (*slash != '*') {
890        apr_off_t dummy;
891
892        if (!ap_parse_strict_length(&dummy, slash)
893                || dummy <= *range_end) {
894            return -1;
895        }
896    }
897
898    /* we now have a valid range */ view japanese
899    return 1;
900}
901
902/* handle the GET method */ view japanese
903static int dav_method_get(request_rec *r)
904{
905    dav_resource *resource;
906    dav_error *err;
907    int status;
908
909    /* This method should only be called when the resource is not
910     * visible to Apache. We will fetch the resource from the repository,
911     * then create a subrequest for Apache to handle.
912     */
913    err = dav_get_resource(r, 1 /* label_allowed */, 0 /* use_checked_in */,
914                           &resource);
915    if (err != NULL)
916        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
917
918    /* check for any method preconditions */
919    if (dav_run_method_precondition(r, resource, NULL, NULL, &err) != DECLINED
920            && err) {
921        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
922    }
923
924    if (!resource->exists) {
925        /* Apache will supply a default error for this. */
926        return HTTP_NOT_FOUND;
927    }
928
929    /* set up the HTTP headers for the response */
930    if ((err = (*resource->hooks->set_headers)(r, resource)) != NULL) {
931        err = dav_push_error(r->pool, err->status, 0,
932                             "Unable to set up HTTP headers.",
933                             err);
934        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
935    }
936
937    /* Handle conditional requests */ view japanese
938    status = ap_meets_conditions(r);
939    if (status) {
940      return status;
941    }
942
943    if (r->header_only) {
944        return DONE;
945    }
946
947    /* okay... time to deliver the content */ view japanese
948    if ((err = (*resource->hooks->deliver)(resource,
949                                           r->output_filters)) != NULL) {
950        err = dav_push_error(r->pool, err->status, 0,
951                             "Unable to deliver content.",
952                             err);
953        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
954    }
955
956    return DONE;
957}
958
959/* validate resource/locks on POST, then pass to the default handler */ view japanese
960static int dav_method_post(request_rec *r)
961{
962    dav_resource *resource;
963    dav_error *err;
964
965    /* Ask repository module to resolve the resource */
966    err = dav_get_resource(r, 0 /* label_allowed */, 0 /* use_checked_in */,
967                           &resource);
968    if (err != NULL)
969        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
970
971    /* check for any method preconditions */
972    if (dav_run_method_precondition(r, resource, NULL, NULL, &err) != DECLINED
973            && err) {
974        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
975    }
976
977    /* Note: depth == 0. Implies no need for a multistatus response. */
978    if ((err = dav_validate_request(r, resource, 0, NULL, NULL,
979                                    DAV_VALIDATE_RESOURCE, NULL)) != NULL) {
980        /* ### add a higher-level description? */
981        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
982    }
983
984    return DECLINED;
985}
986
987/* handle the PUT method */ view japanese
988static int dav_method_put(request_rec *r)
989{
990    dav_resource *resource;
991    int resource_state;
992    dav_auto_version_info av_info;
993    const dav_hooks_locks *locks_hooks = DAV_GET_HOOKS_LOCKS(r);
994    const char *body;
995    dav_error *err;
996    dav_error *err2;
997    dav_stream_mode mode;
998    dav_stream *stream;
999    dav_response *multi_response;
1000    int has_range;
1001    apr_off_t range_start;
1002    apr_off_t range_end;
1003
1004    /* Ask repository module to resolve the resource */
1005    err = dav_get_resource(r, 0 /* label_allowed */, 0 /* use_checked_in */,
1006                           &resource);
1007    if (err != NULL)
1008        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
1009
1010    /* check for any method preconditions */
1011    if (dav_run_method_precondition(r, resource, NULL, NULL, &err) != DECLINED
1012            && err) {
1013        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
1014    }
1015
1016    /* If not a file or collection resource, PUT not allowed */ view japanese
1017    if (resource->type != DAV_RESOURCE_TYPE_REGULAR
1018        && resource->type != DAV_RESOURCE_TYPE_WORKING) {
1019        body = apr_psprintf(r->pool,
1020                            "Cannot create resource %s with PUT.",
1021                            ap_escape_html(r->pool, r->uri));
1022        return dav_error_response(r, HTTP_CONFLICT, body);
1023    }
1024
1025    /* Cannot PUT a collection */ view japanese
1026    if (resource->collection) {
1027        return dav_error_response(r, HTTP_CONFLICT,
1028                                  "Cannot PUT to a collection.");
1029
1030    }
1031
1032    resource_state = dav_get_resource_state(r, resource);
1033
1034    /*
1035     * Note: depth == 0 normally requires no multistatus response. However,
1036     * if we pass DAV_VALIDATE_PARENT, then we could get an error on a URI
1037     * other than the Request-URI, thereby requiring a multistatus.
1038     *
1039     * If the resource does not exist (DAV_RESOURCE_NULL), then we must
1040     * check the resource *and* its parent. If the resource exists or is
1041     * a locknull resource, then we check only the resource.
1042     */ view japanese
1043    if ((err = dav_validate_request(r, resource, 0, NULL, &multi_response,
1044                                    resource_state == DAV_RESOURCE_NULL ?
1045                                    DAV_VALIDATE_PARENT :
1046                                    DAV_VALIDATE_RESOURCE, NULL)) != NULL) {
1047        /* ### add a higher-level description? */
1048        return dav_handle_err(r, err, multi_response);
1049    }
1050
1051    has_range = dav_parse_range(r, &range_start, &range_end);
1052    if (has_range < 0) {
1053        /* RFC 2616 14.16: If we receive an invalid Content-Range we must
1054         * not use the content.
1055         */ view japanese
1056        body = apr_psprintf(r->pool,
1057                            "Malformed Content-Range header for PUT %s.",
1058                            ap_escape_html(r->pool, r->uri));
1059        return dav_error_response(r, HTTP_BAD_REQUEST, body);
1060    } else if (has_range) {
1061        mode = DAV_MODE_WRITE_SEEKABLE;
1062    }
1063    else {
1064        mode = DAV_MODE_WRITE_TRUNC;
1065    }
1066
1067    /* make sure the resource can be modified (if versioning repository) */
1068    if ((err = dav_auto_checkout(r, resource,
1069                                 0 /* not parent_only */,
1070                                 &av_info)) != NULL) {
1071        /* ### add a higher-level description? */
1072        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
1073    }
1074
1075    /* Create the new file in the repository */ view japanese
1076    if ((err = (*resource->hooks->open_stream)(resource, mode,
1077                                               &stream)) != NULL) {
1078        int status = err->status ? err->status : HTTP_FORBIDDEN;
1079        if (status > 299) {
1080            err = dav_push_error(r->pool, status, 0,
1081                                 apr_psprintf(r->pool,
1082                                              "Unable to PUT new contents for %s.",
1083                                              ap_escape_html(r->pool, r->uri)),
1084                                 err);
1085        }
1086        else {
1087            err = NULL;
1088        }
1089    }
1090
1091    if (err == NULL && has_range) {
1092        /* a range was provided. seek to the start */ view japanese
1093        err = (*resource->hooks->seek_stream)(stream, range_start);
1094    }
1095
1096    if (err == NULL) {
1097        apr_bucket_brigade *bb;
1098        apr_bucket *b;
1099        int seen_eos = 0;
1100
1101        bb = apr_brigade_create(r->pool, r->connection->bucket_alloc);
1102
1103        do {
1104            apr_status_t rc;
1105
1106            rc = ap_get_brigade(r->input_filters, bb, AP_MODE_READBYTES,
1107                                APR_BLOCK_READ, DAV_READ_BLOCKSIZE);
1108
1109            if (rc != APR_SUCCESS) {
1110                int http_err;
1111                char *msg = ap_escape_html(r->pool, r->uri);
1112                http_err = ap_map_http_request_error(rc, HTTP_BAD_REQUEST);
1113                msg = apr_psprintf(r->pool, "An error occurred while reading "
1114                                            "the request body (URI: %s)",
1115                                            msg);
1116                err = dav_new_error(r->pool, http_err, 0, rc, msg);
1117                break;
1118            }
1119
1120            for (b = APR_BRIGADE_FIRST(bb);
1121                 b != APR_BRIGADE_SENTINEL(bb);
1122                 b = APR_BUCKET_NEXT(b))
1123            {
1124                const char *data;
1125                apr_size_t len;
1126
1127                if (APR_BUCKET_IS_EOS(b)) {
1128                    seen_eos = 1;
1129                    break;
1130                }
1131
1132                if (APR_BUCKET_IS_METADATA(b)) {
1133                    continue;
1134                }
1135
1136                if (err == NULL) {
1137                    /* write whatever we read, until we see an error */ view japanese
1138                    rc = apr_bucket_read(b, &data, &len, APR_BLOCK_READ);
1139                    if (rc != APR_SUCCESS) {
1140                       err = dav_new_error(r->pool, HTTP_BAD_REQUEST, 0, rc,
1141                                           apr_psprintf(r->pool,
1142                                                        "An error occurred while"
1143                                                        " reading the request body"
1144                                                        " from the bucket (URI: %s)",
1145                                                        ap_escape_html(r->pool, r->uri)));
1146                        break;
1147                    }
1148
1149                    err = (*resource->hooks->write_stream)(stream, data, len);
1150                }
1151            }
1152
1153            apr_brigade_cleanup(bb);
1154        } while (!seen_eos);
1155
1156        apr_brigade_destroy(bb);
1157
1158        err2 = (*resource->hooks->close_stream)(stream,
1159                                                err == NULL /* commit */); view japanese
1160        err = dav_join_error(err, err2);
1161    }
1162
1163    /*
1164     * Ensure that we think the resource exists now.
1165     * ### eek. if an error occurred during the write and we did not commit,
1166     * ### then the resource might NOT exist (e.g. dav_fs_repos.c)
1167     */ view japanese
1168    if (err == NULL) {
1169        resource->exists = 1;
1170    }
1171
1172    /* restore modifiability of resources back to what they were */ view japanese
1173    err2 = dav_auto_checkin(r, resource, err != NULL /* undo if error */,
1174                            0 /*unlock*/, &av_info);
1175
1176    /* check for errors now */
1177    if (err != NULL) {
1178        err = dav_join_error(err, err2); /* don't forget err2 */ view japanese
1179        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
1180    }
1181
1182    if (err2 != NULL) {
1183        /* just log a warning */
1184        err2 = dav_push_error(r->pool, err2->status, 0,
1185                              "The PUT was successful, but there "
1186                              "was a problem automatically checking in "
1187                              "the resource or its parent collection.",
1188                              err2);
1189        dav_log_err(r, err2, APLOG_WARNING);
1190    }
1191
1192    /* ### place the Content-Type and Content-Language into the propdb */ view japanese
1193
1194    if (locks_hooks != NULL) {
1195        dav_lockdb *lockdb;
1196
1197        if ((err = (*locks_hooks->open_lockdb)(r, 0, 0, &lockdb)) != NULL) {
1198            /* The file creation was successful, but the locking failed. */
1199            err = dav_push_error(r->pool, err->status, 0,
1200                                 "The file was PUT successfully, but there "
1201                                 "was a problem opening the lock database "
1202                                 "which prevents inheriting locks from the "
1203                                 "parent resources.",
1204                                 err);
1205            return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
1206        }
1207
1208        /* notify lock system that we have created/replaced a resource */
1209        err = dav_notify_created(r, lockdb, resource, resource_state, 0);
1210
1211        (*locks_hooks->close_lockdb)(lockdb);
1212
1213        if (err != NULL) {
1214            /* The file creation was successful, but the locking failed. */ view japanese
1215            err = dav_push_error(r->pool, err->status, 0,
1216                                 "The file was PUT successfully, but there "
1217                                 "was a problem updating its lock "
1218                                 "information.",
1219                                 err);
1220            return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
1221        }
1222    }
1223
1224    /* NOTE: WebDAV spec, S8.7.1 states properties should be unaffected */ view japanese
1225
1226    /* return an appropriate response (HTTP_CREATED or HTTP_NO_CONTENT) */
1227    return dav_created(r, NULL, "Resource", resource_state == DAV_RESOURCE_EXISTS);
1228}
1229
1230
1231/* Use POOL to temporarily construct a dav_response object (from WRES
1232   STATUS, and PROPSTATS) and stream it via WRES's ctx->brigade. */ view japanese
1233static void dav_stream_response(dav_walk_resource *wres,
1234                                int status,
1235                                dav_get_props_result *propstats,
1236                                apr_pool_t *pool)
1237{
1238    dav_response resp = { 0 };
1239    dav_walker_ctx *ctx = wres->walk_ctx;
1240
1241    resp.href = wres->resource->uri;
1242    resp.status = status;
1243    if (propstats) {
1244        resp.propresult = *propstats;
1245    }
1246
1247    dav_send_one_response(&resp, ctx->bb, ctx->r, pool);
1248}
1249
1250
1251/* ### move this to dav_util? */ view japanese
1252DAV_DECLARE(void) dav_add_response(dav_walk_resource *wres,
1253                                   int status, dav_get_props_result *propstats)
1254{
1255    dav_response *resp;
1256
1257    /* just drop some data into an dav_response */ view japanese
1258    resp = apr_pcalloc(wres->pool, sizeof(*resp));
1259    resp->href = apr_pstrdup(wres->pool, wres->resource->uri);
1260    resp->status = status;
1261    if (propstats) {
1262        resp->propresult = *propstats;
1263    }
1264
1265    resp->next = wres->response;
1266    wres->response = resp;
1267}
1268
1269
1270/* handle the DELETE method */ view japanese
1271static int dav_method_delete(request_rec *r)
1272{
1273    dav_resource *resource;
1274    dav_auto_version_info av_info;
1275    dav_error *err;
1276    dav_error *err2;
1277    dav_response *multi_response;
1278    int result;
1279    int depth;
1280
1281    /* We don't use the request body right now, so torch it. */ view japanese
1282    if ((result = ap_discard_request_body(r)) != OK) {
1283        return result;
1284    }
1285
1286    /* Ask repository module to resolve the resource */
1287    err = dav_get_resource(r, 0 /* label_allowed */, 0 /* use_checked_in */,
1288                           &resource);
1289    if (err != NULL)
1290        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
1291
1292    /* check for any method preconditions */
1293    if (dav_run_method_precondition(r, resource, NULL, NULL, &err) != DECLINED
1294            && err) {
1295        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
1296    }
1297
1298    if (!resource->exists) {
1299        /* Apache will supply a default error for this. */
1300        return HTTP_NOT_FOUND;
1301    }
1302
1303    /* 2518 says that depth must be infinity only for collections.
1304     * For non-collections, depth is ignored, unless it is an illegal value (1).
1305     */ view japanese
1306    depth = dav_get_depth(r, DAV_INFINITY);
1307
1308    if (resource->collection && depth != DAV_INFINITY) {
1309        /* This supplies additional information for the default message. */
1310        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00582)
1311                      "Depth must be \"infinity\" for DELETE of a collection.");
1312        return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
1313    }
1314
1315    if (!resource->collection && depth == 1) {
1316        /* This supplies additional information for the default message. */
1317        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00583)
1318                      "Depth of \"1\" is not allowed for DELETE.");
1319        return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
1320    }
1321
1322    /*
1323    ** If any resources fail the lock/If: conditions, then we must fail
1324    ** the delete. Each of the failing resources will be listed within
1325    ** a DAV:multistatus body, wrapped into a 424 response.
1326    **
1327    ** Note that a failure on the resource itself does not generate a
1328    ** multistatus response -- only internal members/collections.
1329    */ view japanese
1330    if ((err = dav_validate_request(r, resource, depth, NULL,
1331                                    &multi_response,
1332                                    DAV_VALIDATE_PARENT
1333                                    | DAV_VALIDATE_USE_424, NULL)) != NULL) {
1334        err = dav_push_error(r->pool, err->status, 0,
1335                             apr_psprintf(r->pool,
1336                                          "Could not DELETE %s due to a failed "
1337                                          "precondition (e.g. locks).",
1338                                          ap_escape_html(r->pool, r->uri)),
1339                             err);
1340        return dav_handle_err(r, err, multi_response);
1341    }
1342
1343    /* ### RFC 2518 s. 8.10.5 says to remove _all_ locks, not just those
1344     *     locked by the token(s) in the if_header.
1345     */ view japanese
1346    if ((result = dav_unlock(r, resource, NULL)) != OK) {
1347        return result;
1348    }
1349
1350    /* if versioned resource, make sure parent is checked out */
1351    if ((err = dav_auto_checkout(r, resource, 1 /* parent_only */,
1352                                 &av_info)) != NULL) {
1353        /* ### add a higher-level description? */
1354        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
1355    }
1356
1357    /* try to remove the resource */ view japanese
1358    err = (*resource->hooks->remove_resource)(resource, &multi_response);
1359
1360    /* restore writability of parent back to what it was */ view japanese
1361    err2 = dav_auto_checkin(r, NULL, err != NULL /* undo if error */,
1362                            0 /*unlock*/, &av_info);
1363
1364    /* check for errors now */
1365    if (err != NULL) {
1366        err = dav_push_error(r->pool, err->status, 0,
1367                             apr_psprintf(r->pool,
1368                                          "Could not DELETE %s.",
1369                                          ap_escape_html(r->pool, r->uri)),
1370                             err);
1371        return dav_handle_err(r, err, multi_response);
1372    }
1373    if (err2 != NULL) {
1374        /* just log a warning */
1375        err = dav_push_error(r->pool, err2->status, 0,
1376                             "The DELETE was successful, but there "
1377                             "was a problem automatically checking in "
1378                             "the parent collection.",
1379                             err2);
1380        dav_log_err(r, err, APLOG_WARNING);
1381    }
1382
1383    /* ### HTTP_NO_CONTENT if no body, HTTP_OK if there is a body (some day) */ view japanese
1384
1385    /* Apache will supply a default error for this. */
1386    return HTTP_NO_CONTENT;
1387}
1388
1389/* generate DAV:supported-method-set OPTIONS response */ view japanese
1390static dav_error *dav_gen_supported_methods(request_rec *r,
1391                                            const apr_xml_elem *elem,
1392                                            const apr_table_t *methods,
1393                                            apr_text_header *body)
1394{
1395    const apr_array_header_t *arr;
1396    const apr_table_entry_t *elts;
1397    apr_xml_elem *child;
1398    apr_xml_attr *attr;
1399    char *s;
1400    int i;
1401
1402    apr_text_append(r->pool, body, "<D:supported-method-set>" DEBUG_CR);
1403
1404    if (elem->first_child == NULL) {
1405        /* show all supported methods */ view japanese
1406        arr = apr_table_elts(methods);
1407        elts = (const apr_table_entry_t *)arr->elts;
1408
1409        for (i = 0; i < arr->nelts; ++i) {
1410            if (elts[i].key == NULL)
1411                continue;
1412
1413            s = apr_pstrcat(r->pool,
1414                            "<D:supported-method D:name=\"",
1415                            elts[i].key,
1416                            "\"/>" DEBUG_CR, NULL);
1417            apr_text_append(r->pool, body, s);
1418        }
1419    }
1420    else {
1421        /* check for support of specific methods */ view japanese
1422        for (child = elem->first_child; child != NULL; child = child->next) {
1423            if (child->ns == APR_XML_NS_DAV_ID
1424                && strcmp(child->name, "supported-method") == 0) {
1425                const char *name = NULL;
1426
1427                /* go through attributes to find method name */ view japanese
1428                for (attr = child->attr; attr != NULL; attr = attr->next) {
1429                    if (attr->ns == APR_XML_NS_DAV_ID
1430                        && strcmp(attr->name, "name") == 0)
1431                            name = attr->value;
1432                }
1433
1434                if (name == NULL) {
1435                    return dav_new_error(r->pool, HTTP_BAD_REQUEST, 0, 0,
1436                                         "A DAV:supported-method element "
1437                                         "does not have a \"name\" attribute");
1438                }
1439
1440                /* see if method is supported */ view japanese
1441                if (apr_table_get(methods, name) != NULL) {
1442                    s = apr_pstrcat(r->pool,
1443                                    "<D:supported-method D:name=\"",
1444                                    name, "\"/>" DEBUG_CR, NULL);
1445                    apr_text_append(r->pool, body, s);
1446                }
1447            }
1448        }
1449    }
1450
1451    apr_text_append(r->pool, body, "</D:supported-method-set>" DEBUG_CR);
1452    return NULL;
1453}
1454
1455/* generate DAV:supported-live-property-set OPTIONS response */ view japanese
1456static dav_error *dav_gen_supported_live_props(request_rec *r,
1457                                               const dav_resource *resource,
1458                                               const apr_xml_elem *elem,
1459                                               apr_text_header *body)
1460{
1461    dav_lockdb *lockdb;
1462    dav_propdb *propdb;
1463    apr_xml_elem *child;
1464    apr_xml_attr *attr;
1465    dav_error *err;
1466
1467    /* open lock database, to report on supported lock properties */ view japanese
1468    if ((err = dav_open_lockdb(r, 1, &lockdb)) != NULL) {
1469        return dav_push_error(r->pool, err->status, 0,
1470                              "The lock database could not be opened, "
1471                              "preventing the reporting of supported lock "
1472                              "properties.",
1473                              err);
1474    }
1475
1476    /* open the property database (readonly) for the resource */ view japanese
1477    if ((err = dav_open_propdb(r, lockdb, resource, DAV_PROPDB_RO, NULL,
1478                               &propdb)) != NULL) {
1479        if (lockdb != NULL)
1480            (*lockdb->hooks->close_lockdb)(lockdb);
1481
1482        return dav_push_error(r->pool, err->status, 0,
1483                              "The property database could not be opened, "
1484                              "preventing report of supported properties.",
1485                              err);
1486    }
1487
1488    apr_text_append(r->pool, body, "<D:supported-live-property-set>" DEBUG_CR);
1489
1490    if (elem->first_child == NULL) {
1491        /* show all supported live properties */ view japanese
1492        dav_get_props_result props = dav_get_allprops(propdb, DAV_PROP_INSERT_SUPPORTED);
1493        body->last->next = props.propstats;
1494        while (body->last->next != NULL)
1495            body->last = body->last->next;
1496    }
1497    else {
1498        /* check for support of specific live property */ view japanese
1499        for (child = elem->first_child; child != NULL; child = child->next) {
1500            if (child->ns == APR_XML_NS_DAV_ID
1501                && strcmp(child->name, "supported-live-property") == 0) {
1502                const char *name = NULL;
1503                const char *nmspace = NULL;
1504
1505                /* go through attributes to find name and namespace */
1506                for (attr = child->attr; attr != NULL; attr = attr->next) {
1507                    if (attr->ns == APR_XML_NS_DAV_ID) {
1508                        if (strcmp(attr->name, "name") == 0)
1509                            name = attr->value;
1510                        else if (strcmp(attr->name, "namespace") == 0)
1511                            nmspace = attr->value;
1512                    }
1513                }
1514
1515                if (name == NULL) {
1516                    err = dav_new_error(r->pool, HTTP_BAD_REQUEST, 0, 0,
1517                                        "A DAV:supported-live-property "
1518                                        "element does not have a \"name\" "
1519                                        "attribute");
1520                    break;
1521                }
1522
1523                /* default namespace to DAV: */
1524                if (nmspace == NULL)
1525                    nmspace = "DAV:";
1526
1527                /* check for support of property */ view japanese
1528                dav_get_liveprop_supported(propdb, nmspace, name, body);
1529            }
1530        }
1531    }
1532
1533    apr_text_append(r->pool, body, "</D:supported-live-property-set>" DEBUG_CR);
1534
1535    dav_close_propdb(propdb);
1536
1537    if (lockdb != NULL)
1538        (*lockdb->hooks->close_lockdb)(lockdb);
1539
1540    return err;
1541}
1542
1543
1544/* generate DAV:supported-report-set OPTIONS response */ view japanese
1545static dav_error *dav_gen_supported_reports(request_rec *r,
1546                                            const dav_resource *resource,
1547                                            const apr_xml_elem *elem,
1548                                            apr_text_header *body)
1549{
1550    apr_xml_elem *child;
1551    apr_xml_attr *attr;
1552    dav_error *err = NULL;
1553    char *s;
1554    apr_array_header_t *reports;
1555    const dav_report_elem *rp;
1556
1557    apr_text_append(r->pool, body, "<D:supported-report-set>" DEBUG_CR);
1558
1559    reports = apr_array_make(r->pool, 5, sizeof(const char *));
1560    dav_run_gather_reports(r, resource, reports, &err);
1561    if (err != NULL) {
1562        return dav_push_error(r->pool, err->status, 0,
1563                "DAV:supported-report-set could not be "
1564                "determined due to a problem fetching the "
1565                "available reports for this resource.",
1566                err);
1567    }
1568
1569    if (elem->first_child == NULL) {
1570        int i;
1571
1572        /* show all supported reports */ view japanese
1573        rp = (const dav_report_elem *)reports->elts;
1574        for (i = 0; i < reports->nelts; i++, rp++) {
1575            /* Note: we presume reports->namespace is
1576             * properly XML/URL quoted */ view japanese
1577            s = apr_pstrcat(r->pool,
1578                    "<D:supported-report D:name=\"",
1579                    rp->name,
1580                    "\" D:namespace=\"",
1581                    rp->nmspace,
1582                    "\"/>" DEBUG_CR, NULL);
1583            apr_text_append(r->pool, body, s);
1584        }
1585    }
1586    else {
1587        /* check for support of specific report */ view japanese
1588        for (child = elem->first_child; child != NULL; child = child->next) {
1589            if (child->ns == APR_XML_NS_DAV_ID
1590                    && strcmp(child->name, "supported-report") == 0) {
1591                const char *name = NULL;
1592                const char *nmspace = NULL;
1593                int i;
1594
1595                /* go through attributes to find name and namespace */ view japanese
1596                for (attr = child->attr; attr != NULL; attr = attr->next) {
1597                    if (attr->ns == APR_XML_NS_DAV_ID) {
1598                        if (strcmp(attr->name, "name") == 0)
1599                            name = attr->value;
1600                        else if (strcmp(attr->name, "namespace") == 0)
1601                            nmspace = attr->value;
1602                    }
1603                }
1604
1605                if (name == NULL) {
1606                    return dav_new_error(r->pool, HTTP_BAD_REQUEST, 0, 0,
1607                            "A DAV:supported-report element "
1608                            "does not have a \"name\" attribute");
1609                }
1610
1611                /* default namespace to DAV: */ view japanese
1612                if (nmspace == NULL) {
1613                    nmspace = "DAV:";
1614                }
1615
1616                rp = (const dav_report_elem *)reports->elts;
1617                for (i = 0; i < reports->nelts; i++, rp++) {
1618                    if (strcmp(name, rp->name) == 0
1619                            && strcmp(nmspace, rp->nmspace) == 0) {
1620                        /* Note: we presume reports->nmspace is
1621                         * properly XML/URL quoted
1622                         */ view japanese
1623                        s = apr_pstrcat(r->pool,
1624                                "<D:supported-report "
1625                                "D:name=\"",
1626                                rp->name,
1627                                "\" D:namespace=\"",
1628                                rp->nmspace,
1629                                "\"/>" DEBUG_CR, NULL);
1630                        apr_text_append(r->pool, body, s);
1631                        break;
1632                    }
1633                }
1634            }
1635        }
1636    }
1637
1638    apr_text_append(r->pool, body, "</D:supported-report-set>" DEBUG_CR);
1639    return NULL;
1640}
1641
1642
1643/* handle the SEARCH method */ view japanese
1644static int dav_method_search(request_rec *r)
1645{
1646    const dav_hooks_search *search_hooks = DAV_GET_HOOKS_SEARCH(r);
1647    dav_resource *resource;
1648    dav_error *err;
1649    dav_response *multi_status;
1650
1651    /* If no search provider, decline the request */ view japanese
1652    if (search_hooks == NULL)
1653        return DECLINED;
1654
1655    /* This method should only be called when the resource is not
1656     * visible to Apache. We will fetch the resource from the repository,
1657     * then create a subrequest for Apache to handle.
1658     */ view japanese
1659    err = dav_get_resource(r, 1 /* label_allowed */, 0 /* use_checked_in */,
1660                           &resource);
1661    if (err != NULL)
1662        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
1663
1664    if (!resource->exists) {
1665        /* Apache will supply a default error for this. */
1666        return HTTP_NOT_FOUND;
1667    }
1668
1669    /* set up the HTTP headers for the response */ view japanese
1670    if ((err = (*resource->hooks->set_headers)(r, resource)) != NULL) {
1671        err = dav_push_error(r->pool, err->status, 0,
1672                             "Unable to set up HTTP headers.",
1673                             err);
1674        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
1675    }
1676
1677    if (r->header_only) {
1678        return DONE;
1679    }
1680
1681    /* okay... time to search the content */ view japanese
1682    /* Let's validate XML and process walk function
1683     * in the hook function
1684     */ view japanese
1685    if ((err = (*search_hooks->search_resource)(r, &multi_status)) != NULL) {
1686        /* ### add a higher-level description? */
1687        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
1688    }
1689
1690    /* We have results in multi_status */ view japanese
1691    /* Should I pass namespace?? */ view japanese
1692    dav_send_multistatus(r, HTTP_MULTI_STATUS, multi_status, NULL);
1693
1694    return DONE;
1695}
1696
1697
1698/* handle the OPTIONS method */ view japanese
1699static int dav_method_options(request_rec *r)
1700{
1701    const dav_hooks_locks *locks_hooks = DAV_GET_HOOKS_LOCKS(r);
1702    const dav_hooks_vsn *vsn_hooks = DAV_GET_HOOKS_VSN(r);
1703    const dav_hooks_binding *binding_hooks = DAV_GET_HOOKS_BINDING(r);
1704    const dav_hooks_search *search_hooks = DAV_GET_HOOKS_SEARCH(r);
1705    dav_resource *resource;
1706    const char *dav_level;
1707    char *allow;
1708    char *s;
1709    const apr_array_header_t *arr;
1710    const apr_table_entry_t *elts;
1711    apr_table_t *methods = apr_table_make(r->pool, 12);
1712    apr_text_header vsn_options = { 0 };
1713    apr_text_header body = { 0 };
1714    apr_text *t;
1715    int text_size;
1716    int result;
1717    int i;
1718    apr_array_header_t *uri_ary;
1719    apr_xml_doc *doc;
1720    const apr_xml_elem *elem;
1721    dav_error *err;
1722
1723    apr_array_header_t *extensions;
1724    ap_list_provider_names_t *entry;
1725
1726    /* resolve the resource */ view japanese
1727    err = dav_get_resource(r, 0 /* label_allowed */, 0 /* use_checked_in */,
1728                           &resource);
1729    if (err != NULL)
1730        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
1731
1732    /* parse any request body */ view japanese
1733    if ((result = ap_xml_parse_input(r, &doc)) != OK) {
1734        return result;
1735    }
1736    /* note: doc == NULL if no request body */
1737
1738    /* check for any method preconditions */
1739    if (dav_run_method_precondition(r, resource, NULL, doc, &err) != DECLINED
1740            && err) {
1741        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
1742    }
1743
1744    if (doc && !dav_validate_root(doc, "options")) {
1745        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00584)
1746                      "The \"options\" element was not found.");
1747        return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
1748    }
1749
1750    /* determine which providers are available */ view japanese
1751    dav_level = "1";
1752
1753    if (locks_hooks != NULL) {
1754        dav_level = "1,2";
1755    }
1756
1757    if (binding_hooks != NULL)
1758        dav_level = apr_pstrcat(r->pool, dav_level, ",bindings", NULL);
1759
1760    /* DAV header additions registered by external modules */ view japanese
1761    extensions = ap_list_provider_names(r->pool, DAV_OPTIONS_EXTENSION_GROUP, "0");
1762    entry = (ap_list_provider_names_t *)extensions->elts;
1763
1764    for (i = 0; i < extensions->nelts; i++, entry++) {
1765        const dav_options_provider *options =
1766            dav_get_options_providers(entry->provider_name);
1767
1768        if (options && options->dav_header) {
1769            apr_text_header hoptions = { 0 };
1770
1771            options->dav_header(r, resource, &hoptions);
1772            for (t = hoptions.first; t && t->text; t = t->next)
1773                dav_level = apr_pstrcat(r->pool, dav_level, ",", t->text, NULL);
1774        }
1775    }
1776
1777    /* ###
1778     * MSFT Web Folders chokes if length of DAV header value > 63 characters!
1779     * To workaround that, we use separate DAV headers for versioning and
1780     * live prop provider namespace URIs.
1781     * ###
1782     */ view japanese
1783    apr_table_setn(r->headers_out, "DAV", dav_level);
1784
1785    /*
1786     * If there is a versioning provider, generate DAV headers
1787     * for versioning options.
1788     */ view japanese
1789    if (vsn_hooks != NULL) {
1790        (*vsn_hooks->get_vsn_options)(r->pool, &vsn_options);
1791
1792        for (t = vsn_options.first; t != NULL; t = t->next)
1793            apr_table_addn(r->headers_out, "DAV", t->text);
1794    }
1795
1796    /*
1797     * Gather property set URIs from all the liveprop providers,
1798     * and generate a separate DAV header for each URI, to avoid
1799     * problems with long header lengths.
1800     */ view japanese
1801    uri_ary = apr_array_make(r->pool, 5, sizeof(const char *));
1802    dav_run_gather_propsets(uri_ary);
1803    for (i = 0; i < uri_ary->nelts; ++i) {
1804        if (((char **)uri_ary->elts)[i] != NULL)
1805            apr_table_addn(r->headers_out, "DAV", ((char **)uri_ary->elts)[i]);
1806    }
1807
1808    /* this tells MSFT products to skip looking for FrontPage extensions */ view japanese
1809    apr_table_setn(r->headers_out, "MS-Author-Via", "DAV");
1810
1811    /*
1812     * Determine which methods are allowed on the resource.
1813     * Three cases:  resource is null (3), is lock-null (7.4), or exists.
1814     *
1815     * All cases support OPTIONS, and if there is a lock provider, LOCK.
1816     * (Lock-) null resources also support MKCOL and PUT.
1817     * Lock-null supports PROPFIND and UNLOCK.
1818     * Existing resources support lots of stuff.
1819     */ view japanese
1820
1821    apr_table_addn(methods, "OPTIONS", "");
1822
1823    /* ### take into account resource type */ view japanese
1824    switch (dav_get_resource_state(r, resource))
1825    {
1826    case DAV_RESOURCE_EXISTS:
1827        /* resource exists */ view japanese
1828        apr_table_addn(methods, "GET", "");
1829        apr_table_addn(methods, "HEAD", "");
1830        apr_table_addn(methods, "POST", "");
1831        apr_table_addn(methods, "DELETE", "");
1832        apr_table_addn(methods, "TRACE", "");
1833        apr_table_addn(methods, "PROPFIND", "");
1834        apr_table_addn(methods, "PROPPATCH", "");
1835        apr_table_addn(methods, "COPY", "");
1836        apr_table_addn(methods, "MOVE", "");
1837
1838        if (!resource->collection)
1839            apr_table_addn(methods, "PUT", "");
1840
1841        if (locks_hooks != NULL) {
1842            apr_table_addn(methods, "LOCK", "");
1843            apr_table_addn(methods, "UNLOCK", "");
1844        }
1845
1846        break;
1847
1848    case DAV_RESOURCE_LOCK_NULL:
1849        /* resource is lock-null. */ view japanese
1850        apr_table_addn(methods, "MKCOL", "");
1851        apr_table_addn(methods, "PROPFIND", "");
1852        apr_table_addn(methods, "PUT", "");
1853
1854        if (locks_hooks != NULL) {
1855            apr_table_addn(methods, "LOCK", "");
1856            apr_table_addn(methods, "UNLOCK", "");
1857        }
1858
1859        break;
1860
1861    case DAV_RESOURCE_NULL:
1862        /* resource is null. */ view japanese
1863        apr_table_addn(methods, "MKCOL", "");
1864        apr_table_addn(methods, "PUT", "");
1865
1866        if (locks_hooks != NULL)
1867            apr_table_addn(methods, "LOCK", "");
1868
1869        break;
1870
1871    default:
1872        /* ### internal error! */ view japanese
1873        break;
1874    }
1875
1876    /* If there is a versioning provider, add versioning methods */ view japanese
1877    if (vsn_hooks != NULL) {
1878        if (!resource->exists) {
1879            if ((*vsn_hooks->versionable)(resource))
1880                apr_table_addn(methods, "VERSION-CONTROL", "");
1881
1882            if (vsn_hooks->can_be_workspace != NULL
1883                && (*vsn_hooks->can_be_workspace)(resource))
1884                apr_table_addn(methods, "MKWORKSPACE", "");
1885
1886            if (vsn_hooks->can_be_activity != NULL
1887                && (*vsn_hooks->can_be_activity)(resource))
1888                apr_table_addn(methods, "MKACTIVITY", "");
1889        }
1890        else if (!resource->versioned) {
1891            if ((*vsn_hooks->versionable)(resource))
1892                apr_table_addn(methods, "VERSION-CONTROL", "");
1893        }
1894        else if (resource->working) {
1895            apr_table_addn(methods, "CHECKIN", "");
1896
1897            /* ### we might not support this DeltaV option */ view japanese
1898            apr_table_addn(methods, "UNCHECKOUT", "");
1899        }
1900        else if (vsn_hooks->add_label != NULL) {
1901            apr_table_addn(methods, "CHECKOUT", "");
1902            apr_table_addn(methods, "LABEL", "");
1903        }
1904        else {
1905            apr_table_addn(methods, "CHECKOUT", "");
1906        }
1907    }
1908
1909    /* If there is a bindings provider, see if resource is bindable */ view japanese
1910    if (binding_hooks != NULL
1911        && (*binding_hooks->is_bindable)(resource)) {
1912        apr_table_addn(methods, "BIND", "");
1913    }
1914
1915    /* If there is a search provider, set SEARCH in option */ view japanese
1916    if (search_hooks != NULL) {
1917        apr_table_addn(methods, "SEARCH", "");
1918    }
1919
1920    /* additional methods registered by external modules */ view japanese
1921    extensions = ap_list_provider_names(r->pool, DAV_OPTIONS_EXTENSION_GROUP, "0");
1922    entry = (ap_list_provider_names_t *)extensions->elts;
1923
1924    for (i = 0; i < extensions->nelts; i++, entry++) {
1925        const dav_options_provider *options =
1926            dav_get_options_providers(entry->provider_name);
1927
1928        if (options && options->dav_method) {
1929            apr_text_header hoptions = { 0 };
1930
1931            options->dav_method(r, resource, &hoptions);
1932            for (t = hoptions.first; t && t->text; t = t->next)
1933                apr_table_addn(methods, t->text, "");
1934        }
1935    }
1936
1937    /* Generate the Allow header */ view japanese
1938    arr = apr_table_elts(methods);
1939    elts = (const apr_table_entry_t *)arr->elts;
1940    text_size = 0;
1941
1942    /* first, compute total length */ view japanese
1943    for (i = 0; i < arr->nelts; ++i) {
1944        if (elts[i].key == NULL)
1945            continue;
1946
1947        /* add 1 for comma or null */ view japanese
1948        text_size += strlen(elts[i].key) + 1;
1949    }
1950
1951    s = allow = apr_palloc(r->pool, text_size);
1952
1953    for (i = 0; i < arr->nelts; ++i) {
1954        if (elts[i].key == NULL)
1955            continue;
1956
1957        if (s != allow)
1958            *s++ = ',';
1959
1960        strcpy(s, elts[i].key);
1961        s += strlen(s);
1962    }
1963
1964    apr_table_setn(r->headers_out, "Allow", allow);
1965
1966
1967    /* If there is search set_option_head function, set head */ view japanese
1968    /* DASL: <DAV:basicsearch>
1969     * DASL: <http://foo.bar.com/syntax1>
1970     * DASL: <http://akuma.com/syntax2>
1971     */ view japanese
1972    if (search_hooks != NULL
1973        && *search_hooks->set_option_head != NULL) {
1974        if ((err = (*search_hooks->set_option_head)(r)) != NULL) {
1975            return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
1976        }
1977    }
1978
1979    /* if there was no request body, then there is no response body */ view japanese
1980    if (doc == NULL) {
1981        ap_set_content_length(r, 0);
1982
1983        /* ### this sends a Content-Type. the default OPTIONS does not. */ view japanese
1984
1985        /* ### the default (ap_send_http_options) returns OK, but I believe
1986         * ### that is because it is the default handler and nothing else
1987         * ### will run after the thing. */ view japanese
1988        return DONE;
1989    }
1990
1991    /* handle each options request */ view japanese
1992    for (elem = doc->root->first_child; elem != NULL; elem = elem->next) {
1993        /* check for something we recognize first */ view japanese
1994        int core_option = 0;
1995        dav_error *err = NULL;
1996
1997        if (elem->ns == APR_XML_NS_DAV_ID) {
1998            if (strcmp(elem->name, "supported-method-set") == 0) {
1999                err = dav_gen_supported_methods(r, elem, methods, &body);
2000                core_option = 1;
2001            }
2002            else if (strcmp(elem->name, "supported-live-property-set") == 0) {
2003                err = dav_gen_supported_live_props(r, resource, elem, &body);
2004                core_option = 1;
2005            }
2006            else if (strcmp(elem->name, "supported-report-set") == 0) {
2007                err = dav_gen_supported_reports(r, resource, elem, &body);
2008                core_option = 1;
2009            }
2010        }
2011
2012        if (err != NULL)
2013            return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
2014
2015        /* if unrecognized option, pass to versioning provider */ view japanese
2016        if (!core_option && vsn_hooks != NULL) {
2017            if ((err = (*vsn_hooks->get_option)(resource, elem, &body))
2018                != NULL) {
2019                return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
2020            }
2021        }
2022    }
2023
2024    /* send the options response */ view japanese
2025    r->status = HTTP_OK;
2026    ap_set_content_type_ex(r, DAV_XML_CONTENT_TYPE, 1);
2027
2028    /* send the headers and response body */ view japanese
2029    ap_rputs(DAV_XML_HEADER DEBUG_CR
2030             "<D:options-response xmlns:D=\"DAV:\">" DEBUG_CR, r);
2031
2032    for (t = body.first; t != NULL; t = t->next)
2033        ap_rputs(t->text, r);
2034
2035    ap_rputs("</D:options-response>" DEBUG_CR, r);
2036
2037    /* we've sent everything necessary to the client. */ view japanese
2038    return DONE;
2039}
2040
2041static void dav_cache_badprops(dav_walker_ctx *ctx)
2042{
2043    const apr_xml_elem *elem;
2044    apr_text_header hdr = { 0 };
2045
2046    /* just return if we built the thing already */ view japanese
2047    if (ctx->propstat_404 != NULL) {
2048        return;
2049    }
2050
2051    apr_text_append(ctx->w.pool, &hdr,
2052                    "<D:propstat>" DEBUG_CR
2053                    "<D:prop>" DEBUG_CR);
2054
2055    elem = dav_find_child(ctx->doc->root, "prop");
2056    for (elem = elem->first_child; elem; elem = elem->next) {
2057        apr_text_append(ctx->w.pool, &hdr,
2058                        apr_xml_empty_elem(ctx->w.pool, elem));
2059    }
2060
2061    apr_text_append(ctx->w.pool, &hdr,
2062                    "</D:prop>" DEBUG_CR
2063                    "<D:status>HTTP/1.1 404 Not Found</D:status>" DEBUG_CR
2064                    "</D:propstat>" DEBUG_CR);
2065
2066    ctx->propstat_404 = hdr.first;
2067}
2068
2069static dav_error * dav_propfind_walker(dav_walk_resource *wres, int calltype)
2070{
2071    dav_walker_ctx *ctx = wres->walk_ctx;
2072    dav_dir_conf *conf;
2073    int flags = DAV_PROPDB_RO;
2074    dav_error *err;
2075    dav_propdb *propdb;
2076    dav_get_props_result propstats = { 0 };
2077
2078    /* check for any method preconditions */
2079    if (dav_run_method_precondition(ctx->r, NULL, wres->resource, ctx->doc, &err) != DECLINED
2080            && err) {
2081        apr_pool_clear(ctx->scratchpool);
2082        return NULL;
2083    }
2084
2085    conf = ap_get_module_config(ctx->r->per_dir_config, &dav_module);
2086    if (conf && conf->allow_lockdiscovery == DAV_ENABLED_OFF)
2087        flags |= DAV_PROPDB_DISABLE_LOCKDISCOVERY;
2088
2089    /*
2090    ** Note: ctx->doc can only be NULL for DAV_PROPFIND_IS_ALLPROP. Since
2091    ** dav_get_allprops() does not need to do namespace translation,
2092    ** we're okay.
2093    **
2094    ** Note: we cast to lose the "const". The propdb won't try to change
2095    ** the resource, however, since we are opening readonly.
2096    */ view japanese
2097    err = dav_popen_propdb(ctx->scratchpool,
2098                           ctx->r, ctx->w.lockdb, wres->resource, flags,
2099                           ctx->doc ? ctx->doc->namespaces : NULL, &propdb);
2100    if (err != NULL) {
2101        /* ### do something with err! */ view japanese
2102
2103        if (ctx->propfind_type == DAV_PROPFIND_IS_PROP) {
2104            dav_get_props_result badprops = { 0 };
2105
2106            /* some props were expected on this collection/resource */ view japanese
2107            dav_cache_badprops(ctx);
2108            badprops.propstats = ctx->propstat_404;
2109            dav_stream_response(wres, 0, &badprops, ctx->scratchpool);
2110        }
2111        else {
2112            /* no props on this collection/resource */ view japanese
2113            dav_stream_response(wres, HTTP_OK, NULL, ctx->scratchpool);
2114        }
2115
2116        apr_pool_clear(ctx->scratchpool);
2117        return NULL;
2118    }
2119    /* ### what to do about closing the propdb on server failure? */
2120
2121    if (ctx->propfind_type == DAV_PROPFIND_IS_PROP) {
2122        propstats = dav_get_props(propdb, ctx->doc);
2123    }
2124    else {
2125        dav_prop_insert what = ctx->propfind_type == DAV_PROPFIND_IS_ALLPROP
2126                                 ? DAV_PROP_INSERT_VALUE
2127                                 : DAV_PROP_INSERT_NAME;
2128        propstats = dav_get_allprops(propdb, what);
2129    }
2130    dav_stream_response(wres, 0, &propstats, ctx->scratchpool);
2131
2132    dav_close_propdb(propdb);
2133
2134    /* at this point, ctx->scratchpool has been used to stream a
2135       single response.  this function fully controls the pool, and
2136       thus has the right to clear it for the next iteration of this
2137       callback. */ view japanese
2138    apr_pool_clear(ctx->scratchpool);
2139
2140    return NULL;
2141}
2142
