2143/* handle the PROPFIND method */ view japanese
2144static int dav_method_propfind(request_rec *r)
2145{
2146    dav_resource *resource;
2147    int depth;
2148    dav_error *err;
2149    int result;
2150    apr_xml_doc *doc;
2151    dav_walker_ctx ctx = { { 0 } };
2152    dav_response *multi_status;
2153
2154    /* Ask repository module to resolve the resource */
2155    err = dav_get_resource(r, 1 /* label_allowed */, 0 /* use_checked_in */,
2156                           &resource);
2157    if (err != NULL)
2158        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
2159
2160    if ((result = ap_xml_parse_input(r, &doc)) != OK) {
2161        return result;
2162    }
2163    /* note: doc == NULL if no request body */
2164
2165    /* check for any method preconditions */
2166    if (dav_run_method_precondition(r, resource, NULL, doc, &err) != DECLINED
2167            && err) {
2168        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
2169    }
2170
2171    if (dav_get_resource_state(r, resource) == DAV_RESOURCE_NULL) {
2172        /* Apache will supply a default error for this. */
2173        return HTTP_NOT_FOUND;
2174    }
2175
2176    if ((depth = dav_get_depth(r, DAV_INFINITY)) < 0) {
2177        /* dav_get_depth() supplies additional information for the
2178         * default message. */
2179        return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
2180    }
2181
2182    if (depth == DAV_INFINITY && resource->collection) {
2183        dav_dir_conf *conf;
2184        conf = (dav_dir_conf *)ap_get_module_config(r->per_dir_config,
2185                                                    &dav_module);
2186        /* default is to DISALLOW these requests */ view japanese
2187        if (conf->allow_depthinfinity != DAV_ENABLED_ON) {
2188            return dav_error_response(r, HTTP_FORBIDDEN,
2189                                      apr_psprintf(r->pool,
2190                                                   "PROPFIND requests with a "
2191                                                   "Depth of \"infinity\" are "
2192                                                   "not allowed for %s.",
2193                                                   ap_escape_html(r->pool,
2194                                                                  r->uri)));
2195        }
2196    }
2197
2198    if (doc && !dav_validate_root(doc, "propfind")) {
2199        /* This supplies additional information for the default message. */
2200        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00585)
2201                      "The \"propfind\" element was not found.");
2202        return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
2203    }
2204
2205    /* ### validate that only one of these three elements is present */ view japanese
2206
2207    if (doc == NULL || dav_find_child(doc->root, "allprop") != NULL) {
2208        /* note: no request body implies allprop */ view japanese
2209        ctx.propfind_type = DAV_PROPFIND_IS_ALLPROP;
2210    }
2211    else if (dav_find_child(doc->root, "propname") != NULL) {
2212        ctx.propfind_type = DAV_PROPFIND_IS_PROPNAME;
2213    }
2214    else if (dav_find_child(doc->root, "prop") != NULL) {
2215        ctx.propfind_type = DAV_PROPFIND_IS_PROP;
2216    }
2217    else {
2218        /* "propfind" element must have one of the above three children */ view japanese
2219
2220        /* This supplies additional information for the default message. */
2221        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00586)
2222                      "The \"propfind\" element does not contain one of "
2223                      "the required child elements (the specific command).");
2224        return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
2225    }
2226
2227    ctx.w.walk_type = DAV_WALKTYPE_NORMAL | DAV_WALKTYPE_AUTH | DAV_WALKTYPE_TOLERANT;
2228    ctx.w.func = dav_propfind_walker;
2229    ctx.w.walk_ctx = &ctx;
2230    ctx.w.pool = r->pool;
2231    ctx.w.root = resource;
2232
2233    ctx.doc = doc;
2234    ctx.r = r;
2235    ctx.bb = apr_brigade_create(r->pool, r->connection->bucket_alloc);
2236    apr_pool_create(&ctx.scratchpool, r->pool);
2237    apr_pool_tag(ctx.scratchpool, "mod_dav-scratch");
2238
2239    if ((err = dav_open_lockdb(r, 1, &ctx.w.lockdb)) != NULL) {
2240        err = dav_push_error(r->pool, err->status, 0,
2241                             "The lock database could not be opened, "
2242                             "preventing access to the various lock "
2243                             "properties for the PROPFIND.",
2244                             err);
2245        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
2246    }
2247    if (ctx.w.lockdb != NULL) {
2248        /* if we have a lock database, then we can walk locknull resources */ view japanese
2249        ctx.w.walk_type |= DAV_WALKTYPE_LOCKNULL;
2250    }
2251
2252    /* send <multistatus> tag, with all doc->namespaces attached.  */ view japanese
2253
2254    /* NOTE: we *cannot* leave out the doc's namespaces from the
2255       initial <multistatus> tag.  if a 404 was generated for an HREF,
2256       then we need to spit out the doc's namespaces for use by the
2257       404. Note that <response> elements will override these ns0,
2258       ns1, etc, but NOT within the <response> scope for the
2259       badprops. */ view japanese
2260    dav_begin_multistatus(ctx.bb, r, HTTP_MULTI_STATUS,
2261                          doc ? doc->namespaces : NULL);
2262
2263    /* Have the provider walk the resource. */ view japanese
2264    err = (*resource->hooks->walk)(&ctx.w, depth, &multi_status);
2265
2266    if (ctx.w.lockdb != NULL) {
2267        (*ctx.w.lockdb->hooks->close_lockdb)(ctx.w.lockdb);
2268    }
2269
2270    if (err != NULL) {
2271        /* If an error occurred during the resource walk, there's
2272           basically nothing we can do but abort the connection and
2273           log an error.  This is one of the limitations of HTTP; it
2274           needs to "know" the entire status of the response before
2275           generating it, which is just impossible in these streamy
2276           response situations. */ view japanese
2277        err = dav_push_error(r->pool, err->status, 0,
2278                             "Provider encountered an error while streaming"
2279                             " a multistatus PROPFIND response.", err);
2280        dav_log_err(r, err, APLOG_ERR);
2281        r->connection->aborted = 1;
2282        return DONE;
2283    }
2284
2285    dav_finish_multistatus(r, ctx.bb);
2286
2287    /* the response has been sent. */
2288    return DONE;
2289}
2290
2291DAV_DECLARE(apr_text *) dav_failed_proppatch(apr_pool_t *p,
2292                                             apr_array_header_t *prop_ctx)
2293{
2294    apr_text_header hdr = { 0 };
2295    int i = prop_ctx->nelts;
2296    dav_prop_ctx *ctx = (dav_prop_ctx *)prop_ctx->elts;
2297    dav_error *err424_set = NULL;
2298    dav_error *err424_delete = NULL;
2299    const char *s;
2300
2301    /* ### might be nice to sort by status code and description */ view japanese
2302
2303    for ( ; i-- > 0; ++ctx ) {
2304        apr_text_append(p, &hdr,
2305                        "<D:propstat>" DEBUG_CR
2306                        "<D:prop>");
2307        apr_text_append(p, &hdr, apr_xml_empty_elem(p, ctx->prop));
2308        apr_text_append(p, &hdr, "</D:prop>" DEBUG_CR);
2309
2310        if (ctx->err == NULL) {
2311            /* nothing was assigned here yet, so make it a 424 */ view japanese
2312
2313            if (ctx->operation == DAV_PROP_OP_SET) {
2314                if (err424_set == NULL)
2315                    err424_set = dav_new_error(p, HTTP_FAILED_DEPENDENCY, 0, 0,
2316                                               "Attempted DAV:set operation "
2317                                               "could not be completed due "
2318                                               "to other errors.");
2319                ctx->err = err424_set;
2320            }
2321            else if (ctx->operation == DAV_PROP_OP_DELETE) {
2322                if (err424_delete == NULL)
2323                    err424_delete = dav_new_error(p, HTTP_FAILED_DEPENDENCY, 0, 0,
2324                                                  "Attempted DAV:remove "
2325                                                  "operation could not be "
2326                                                  "completed due to other "
2327                                                  "errors.");
2328                ctx->err = err424_delete;
2329            }
2330        }
2331
2332        s = apr_psprintf(p,
2333                         "<D:status>"
2334                         "HTTP/1.1 %d (status)"
2335                         "</D:status>" DEBUG_CR,
2336                         ctx->err->status);
2337        apr_text_append(p, &hdr, s);
2338
2339        /* ### we should use compute_desc if necessary... */ view japanese
2340        if (ctx->err->desc != NULL) {
2341            apr_text_append(p, &hdr, "<D:responsedescription>" DEBUG_CR);
2342            apr_text_append(p, &hdr, ctx->err->desc);
2343            apr_text_append(p, &hdr, "</D:responsedescription>" DEBUG_CR);
2344        }
2345
2346        apr_text_append(p, &hdr, "</D:propstat>" DEBUG_CR);
2347    }
2348
2349    return hdr.first;
2350}
2351
2352DAV_DECLARE(apr_text *) dav_success_proppatch(apr_pool_t *p,
2353                                              apr_array_header_t *prop_ctx)
2354{
2355    apr_text_header hdr = { 0 };
2356    int i = prop_ctx->nelts;
2357    dav_prop_ctx *ctx = (dav_prop_ctx *)prop_ctx->elts;
2358
2359    /*
2360     * ### we probably need to revise the way we assemble the response...
2361     * ### this code assumes everything will return status==200.
2362     */ view japanese
2363
2364    apr_text_append(p, &hdr,
2365                    "<D:propstat>" DEBUG_CR
2366                    "<D:prop>" DEBUG_CR);
2367
2368    for ( ; i-- > 0; ++ctx ) {
2369        apr_text_append(p, &hdr, apr_xml_empty_elem(p, ctx->prop));
2370    }
2371
2372    apr_text_append(p, &hdr,
2373                   "</D:prop>" DEBUG_CR
2374                   "<D:status>HTTP/1.1 200 OK</D:status>" DEBUG_CR
2375                   "</D:propstat>" DEBUG_CR);
2376
2377    return hdr.first;
2378}
2379
2380static void dav_prop_log_errors(dav_prop_ctx *ctx)
2381{
2382    dav_log_err(ctx->r, ctx->err, APLOG_ERR);
2383}
2384
2385/*
2386 * Call <func> for each context. This can stop when an error occurs, or
2387 * simply iterate through the whole list.
2388 *
2389 * Returns 1 if an error occurs (and the iteration is aborted). Returns 0
2390 * if all elements are processed.
2391 *
2392 * If <reverse> is true (non-zero), then the list is traversed in
2393 * reverse order.
2394 */ view japanese
2395static int dav_process_ctx_list(void (*func)(dav_prop_ctx *ctx),
2396                                apr_array_header_t *ctx_list, int stop_on_error,
2397                                int reverse)
2398{
2399    int i = ctx_list->nelts;
2400    dav_prop_ctx *ctx = (dav_prop_ctx *)ctx_list->elts;
2401
2402    if (reverse)
2403        ctx += i;
2404
2405    while (i--) {
2406        if (reverse)
2407            --ctx;
2408
2409        (*func)(ctx);
2410        if (stop_on_error && DAV_PROP_CTX_HAS_ERR(*ctx)) {
2411            return 1;
2412        }
2413
2414        if (!reverse)
2415            ++ctx;
2416    }
2417
2418    return 0;
2419}
2420
2421/* handle the PROPPATCH method */ view japanese
2422static int dav_method_proppatch(request_rec *r)
2423{
2424    dav_error *err;
2425    dav_resource *resource;
2426    int result;
2427    apr_xml_doc *doc;
2428    apr_xml_elem *child;
2429    dav_propdb *propdb;
2430    int failure = 0;
2431    dav_response resp = { 0 };
2432    apr_text *propstat_text;
2433    apr_array_header_t *ctx_list;
2434    dav_prop_ctx *ctx;
2435    dav_auto_version_info av_info;
2436
2437    /* Ask repository module to resolve the resource */
2438    err = dav_get_resource(r, 0 /* label_allowed */, 0 /* use_checked_in */,
2439                           &resource);
2440    if (err != NULL)
2441        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
2442
2443    if ((result = ap_xml_parse_input(r, &doc)) != OK) {
2444        return result;
2445    }
2446    /* note: doc == NULL if no request body */
2447
2448    /* check for any method preconditions */
2449    if (dav_run_method_precondition(r, resource, NULL, doc, &err) != DECLINED
2450            && err) {
2451        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
2452    }
2453
2454    if (!resource->exists) {
2455        /* Apache will supply a default error for this. */
2456        return HTTP_NOT_FOUND;
2457    }
2458
2459    if (doc == NULL || !dav_validate_root(doc, "propertyupdate")) {
2460        /* This supplies additional information for the default message. */
2461        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00587)
2462                      "The request body does not contain "
2463                      "a \"propertyupdate\" element.");
2464        return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
2465    }
2466
2467    /* Check If-Headers and existing locks */
2468    /* Note: depth == 0. Implies no need for a multistatus response. */
2469    if ((err = dav_validate_request(r, resource, 0, NULL, NULL,
2470                                    DAV_VALIDATE_RESOURCE, NULL)) != NULL) {
2471        /* ### add a higher-level description? */
2472        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
2473    }
2474
2475    /* make sure the resource can be modified (if versioning repository) */ view japanese
2476    if ((err = dav_auto_checkout(r, resource,
2477                                 0 /* not parent_only */, view japanese
2478                                 &av_info)) != NULL) {
2479        /* ### add a higher-level description? */
2480        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
2481    }
2482
2483    if ((err = dav_open_propdb(r, NULL, resource,
2484                               DAV_PROPDB_NONE, doc->namespaces,
2485                               &propdb)) != NULL) {
2486        /* undo any auto-checkout */
2487        dav_auto_checkin(r, resource, 1 /*undo*/, 0 /*unlock*/, &av_info);
2488
2489        err = dav_push_error(r->pool, HTTP_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, 0,
2490                             apr_psprintf(r->pool,
2491                                          "Could not open the property "
2492                                          "database for %s.",
2493                                          ap_escape_html(r->pool, r->uri)),
2494                             err);
2495        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
2496    }
2497    /* ### what to do about closing the propdb on server failure? */ view japanese
2498
2499    /* ### validate "live" properties */ view japanese
2500
2501    /* set up an array to hold property operation contexts */ view japanese
2502    ctx_list = apr_array_make(r->pool, 10, sizeof(dav_prop_ctx));
2503
2504    /* do a first pass to ensure that all "remove" properties exist */ view japanese
2505    for (child = doc->root->first_child; child; child = child->next) {
2506        int is_remove;
2507        apr_xml_elem *prop_group;
2508        apr_xml_elem *one_prop;
2509
2510        /* Ignore children that are not set/remove */ view japanese
2511        if (child->ns != APR_XML_NS_DAV_ID
2512            || (!(is_remove = (strcmp(child->name, "remove") == 0))
2513                && strcmp(child->name, "set") != 0)) {
2514            continue;
2515        }
2516
2517        /* make sure that a "prop" child exists for set/remove */ view japanese
2518        if ((prop_group = dav_find_child(child, "prop")) == NULL) {
2519            dav_close_propdb(propdb);
2520
2521            /* undo any auto-checkout */ view japanese
2522            dav_auto_checkin(r, resource, 1 /*undo*/, 0 /*unlock*/, &av_info);
2523
2524            /* This supplies additional information for the default message. */
2525            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00588)
2526                          "A \"prop\" element is missing inside "
2527                          "the propertyupdate command.");
2528            return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
2529        }
2530
2531        for (one_prop = prop_group->first_child; one_prop;
2532             one_prop = one_prop->next) {
2533
2534            ctx = (dav_prop_ctx *)apr_array_push(ctx_list);
2535            ctx->propdb = propdb;
2536            ctx->operation = is_remove ? DAV_PROP_OP_DELETE : DAV_PROP_OP_SET;
2537            ctx->prop = one_prop;
2538
2539            ctx->r = r;         /* for later use by dav_prop_log_errors() */ view japanese
2540
2541            dav_prop_validate(ctx);
2542
2543            if ( DAV_PROP_CTX_HAS_ERR(*ctx) ) {
2544                failure = 1;
2545            }
2546        }
2547    }
2548
2549    /* ### should test that we found at least one set/remove */ view japanese
2550
2551    /* execute all of the operations */ view japanese
2552    if (!failure && dav_process_ctx_list(dav_prop_exec, ctx_list, 1, 0)) {
2553        failure = 1;
2554    }
2555
2556    /* generate a failure/success response */ view japanese
2557    if (failure) {
2558        (void)dav_process_ctx_list(dav_prop_rollback, ctx_list, 0, 1);
2559        propstat_text = dav_failed_proppatch(r->pool, ctx_list);
2560    }
2561    else {
2562        (void)dav_process_ctx_list(dav_prop_commit, ctx_list, 0, 0);
2563        propstat_text = dav_success_proppatch(r->pool, ctx_list);
2564    }
2565
2566    /* make sure this gets closed! */ view japanese
2567    dav_close_propdb(propdb);
2568
2569    /* complete any auto-versioning */ view japanese
2570    dav_auto_checkin(r, resource, failure, 0 /*unlock*/, &av_info);
2571
2572    /* log any errors that occurred */ view japanese
2573    (void)dav_process_ctx_list(dav_prop_log_errors, ctx_list, 0, 0);
2574
2575    resp.href = resource->uri;
2576
2577    /* ### should probably use something new to pass along this text... */ view japanese
2578    resp.propresult.propstats = propstat_text;
2579
2580    dav_send_multistatus(r, HTTP_MULTI_STATUS, &resp, doc->namespaces);
2581
2582    /* the response has been sent. */
2583    return DONE;
2584}
2585
2586static int process_mkcol_body(request_rec *r)
2587{
2588    /* This is snarfed from ap_setup_client_block(). We could get pretty
2589     * close to this behavior by passing REQUEST_NO_BODY, but we need to
2590     * return HTTP_UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE (while ap_setup_client_block
2591     * returns HTTP_REQUEST_ENTITY_TOO_LARGE). */ view japanese
2592
2593    const char *tenc = apr_table_get(r->headers_in, "Transfer-Encoding");
2594    const char *lenp = apr_table_get(r->headers_in, "Content-Length");
2595
2596    /* make sure to set the Apache request fields properly. */ view japanese
2597    r->read_body = REQUEST_NO_BODY;
2598    r->read_chunked = 0;
2599    r->remaining = 0;
2600
2601    if (tenc) {
2602        if (ap_cstr_casecmp(tenc, "chunked")) {
2603            /* Use this instead of Apache's default error string */ view japanese
2604            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00589)
2605                          "Unknown Transfer-Encoding %s", tenc);
2606            return HTTP_NOT_IMPLEMENTED;
2607        }
2608
2609        r->read_chunked = 1;
2610    }
2611    else if (lenp) {
2612        if (!ap_parse_strict_length(&r->remaining, lenp)) {
2613            r->remaining = 0;
2614            /* This supplies additional information for the default message. */
2615            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00590)
2616                          "Invalid Content-Length %s", lenp);
2617            return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
2618        }
2619    }
2620
2621    if (r->read_chunked || r->remaining > 0) {
2622        /* ### log something? */ view japanese
2623
2624        /* Apache will supply a default error for this. */
2625        return HTTP_UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE;
2626    }
2627
2628    /*
2629     * Get rid of the body. this will call ap_setup_client_block(), but
2630     * our copy above has already verified its work.
2631     */ view japanese
2632    return ap_discard_request_body(r);
2633}
2634
2635/* handle the MKCOL method */ view japanese
2636static int dav_method_mkcol(request_rec *r)
2637{
2638    dav_resource *resource;
2639    int resource_state;
2640    dav_auto_version_info av_info;
2641    const dav_hooks_locks *locks_hooks = DAV_GET_HOOKS_LOCKS(r);
2642    dav_error *err;
2643    dav_error *err2;
2644    int result;
2645    dav_response *multi_status;
2646
2647    /* handle the request body */ view japanese
2648    /* ### this may move lower once we start processing bodies */ view japanese
2649    if ((result = process_mkcol_body(r)) != OK) {
2650        return result;
2651    }
2652
2653    /* Ask repository module to resolve the resource */
2654    err = dav_get_resource(r, 0 /* label_allowed */, 0 /* use_checked_in */,
2655                           &resource);
2656    if (err != NULL)
2657        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
2658
2659    /* check for any method preconditions */
2660    if (dav_run_method_precondition(r, resource, NULL, NULL, &err) != DECLINED
2661            && err) {
2662        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
2663    }
2664
2665    if (resource->exists) {
2666        /* oops. something was already there! */ view japanese
2667
2668        /* Apache will supply a default error for this. */
2669        /* ### we should provide a specific error message! */ view japanese
2670        return HTTP_METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED;
2671    }
2672
2673    resource_state = dav_get_resource_state(r, resource);
2674
2675    /*
2676     * Check If-Headers and existing locks.
2677     *
2678     * Note: depth == 0 normally requires no multistatus response. However,
2679     * if we pass DAV_VALIDATE_PARENT, then we could get an error on a URI
2680     * other than the Request-URI, thereby requiring a multistatus.
2681     *
2682     * If the resource does not exist (DAV_RESOURCE_NULL), then we must
2683     * check the resource *and* its parent. If the resource exists or is
2684     * a locknull resource, then we check only the resource.
2685     */ view japanese
2686    if ((err = dav_validate_request(r, resource, 0, NULL, &multi_status,
2687                                    resource_state == DAV_RESOURCE_NULL ?
2688                                    DAV_VALIDATE_PARENT :
2689                                    DAV_VALIDATE_RESOURCE, NULL)) != NULL) {
2690        /* ### add a higher-level description? */
2691        return dav_handle_err(r, err, multi_status);
2692    }
2693
2694    /* if versioned resource, make sure parent is checked out */ view japanese
2695    if ((err = dav_auto_checkout(r, resource, 1 /* parent_only */,
2696                                 &av_info)) != NULL) {
2697        /* ### add a higher-level description? */
2698        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
2699    }
2700
2701    /* try to create the collection */ view japanese
2702    resource->collection = 1;
2703    err = (*resource->hooks->create_collection)(resource);
2704
2705    /* restore modifiability of parent back to what it was */ view japanese
2706    err2 = dav_auto_checkin(r, NULL, err != NULL /* undo if error */,
2707                            0 /*unlock*/, &av_info);
2708
2709    /* check for errors now */ view japanese
2710    if (err != NULL) {
2711        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
2712    }
2713    if (err2 != NULL) {
2714        /* just log a warning */
2715        err = dav_push_error(r->pool, err2->status, 0,
2716                             "The MKCOL was successful, but there "
2717                             "was a problem automatically checking in "
2718                             "the parent collection.",
2719                             err2);
2720        dav_log_err(r, err, APLOG_WARNING);
2721    }
2722
2723    if (locks_hooks != NULL) {
2724        dav_lockdb *lockdb;
2725
2726        if ((err = (*locks_hooks->open_lockdb)(r, 0, 0, &lockdb)) != NULL) {
2727            /* The directory creation was successful, but the locking failed. */ view japanese
2728            err = dav_push_error(r->pool, err->status, 0,
2729                                 "The MKCOL was successful, but there "
2730                                 "was a problem opening the lock database "
2731                                 "which prevents inheriting locks from the "
2732                                 "parent resources.",
2733                                 err);
2734            return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
2735        }
2736
2737        /* notify lock system that we have created/replaced a resource */
2738        err = dav_notify_created(r, lockdb, resource, resource_state, 0);
2739
2740        (*locks_hooks->close_lockdb)(lockdb);
2741
2742        if (err != NULL) {
2743            /* The dir creation was successful, but the locking failed. */
2744            err = dav_push_error(r->pool, err->status, 0,
2745                                 "The MKCOL was successful, but there "
2746                                 "was a problem updating its lock "
2747                                 "information.",
2748                                 err);
2749            return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
2750        }
2751    }
2752
2753    /* return an appropriate response (HTTP_CREATED) */
2754    return dav_created(r, NULL, "Collection", 0);
2755}
2756
2757/* handle the COPY and MOVE methods */ view japanese
2758static int dav_method_copymove(request_rec *r, int is_move)
2759{
2760    dav_resource *resource;
2761    dav_resource *resnew;
2762    dav_auto_version_info src_av_info = { 0 };
2763    dav_auto_version_info dst_av_info = { 0 };
2764    const char *body;
2765    const char *dest;
2766    dav_error *err;
2767    dav_error *err2;
2768    dav_error *err3;
2769    dav_response *multi_response;
2770    dav_lookup_result lookup;
2771    int is_dir;
2772    int overwrite;
2773    int depth;
2774    int result;
2775    dav_lockdb *lockdb;
2776    int replace_dest;
2777    int resnew_state;
2778
2779    /* Ask repository module to resolve the resource */
2780    err = dav_get_resource(r, !is_move /* label_allowed */,
2781                           0 /* use_checked_in */, &resource);
2782    if (err != NULL)
2783        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
2784
2785    /* check for any method preconditions */
2786    if (dav_run_method_precondition(r, resource, NULL, NULL, &err) != DECLINED
2787            && err) {
2788        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
2789    }
2790
2791    if (!resource->exists) {
2792        /* Apache will supply a default error for this. */
2793        return HTTP_NOT_FOUND;
2794    }
2795
2796    /* If not a file or collection resource, COPY/MOVE not allowed */ view japanese
2797    /* ### allow COPY/MOVE of DeltaV resource types */ view japanese
2798    if (resource->type != DAV_RESOURCE_TYPE_REGULAR) {
2799        body = apr_psprintf(r->pool,
2800                            "Cannot COPY/MOVE resource %s.",
2801                            ap_escape_html(r->pool, r->uri));
2802        return dav_error_response(r, HTTP_METHOD_NOT_ALLOWED, body);
2803    }
2804
2805    /* get the destination URI */
2806    dest = apr_table_get(r->headers_in, "Destination");
2807    if (dest == NULL) {
2808        /* Look in headers provided by Netscape's Roaming Profiles */ view japanese
2809        const char *nscp_host = apr_table_get(r->headers_in, "Host");
2810        const char *nscp_path = apr_table_get(r->headers_in, "New-uri");
2811
2812        if (nscp_host != NULL && nscp_path != NULL)
2813            dest = apr_pstrcat(r->pool, "http://", nscp_host, nscp_path, NULL); view japanese
2814    }
2815    if (dest == NULL) {
2816        /* This supplies additional information for the default message. */
2817        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00591)
2818                      "The request is missing a Destination header.");
2819        return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
2820    }
2821
2822    lookup = dav_lookup_uri(dest, r, 1 /* must_be_absolute */);
2823    if (lookup.rnew == NULL) {
2824        if (lookup.err.status == HTTP_BAD_REQUEST) {
2825            /* This supplies additional information for the default message. */
2826            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00592)
2827                          "%s", lookup.err.desc);
2828            return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
2829        }
2830
2831        /* ### this assumes that dav_lookup_uri() only generates a status
2832         * ### that Apache can provide a status line for!! */
2833
2834        return dav_error_response(r, lookup.err.status, lookup.err.desc);
2835    }
2836    if (lookup.rnew->status != HTTP_OK) {
2837        const char *auth = apr_table_get(lookup.rnew->err_headers_out,
2838                                        "WWW-Authenticate");
2839        if (lookup.rnew->status == HTTP_UNAUTHORIZED && auth != NULL) {
2840            /* propagate the WWW-Authorization header up from the
2841             * subreq so the client sees it. */ view japanese
2842            apr_table_setn(r->err_headers_out, "WWW-Authenticate",
2843                           apr_pstrdup(r->pool, auth));
2844        }
2845
2846        /* ### how best to report this... */
2847        return dav_error_response(r, lookup.rnew->status,
2848                                  "Destination URI had an error.");
2849    }
2850
2851    /* Resolve destination resource */ view japanese
2852    err = dav_get_resource(lookup.rnew, 0 /* label_allowed */,
2853                           0 /* use_checked_in */, &resnew);
2854    if (err != NULL)
2855        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
2856
2857    /* check for any method preconditions */
2858    if (dav_run_method_precondition(r, resource, resnew, NULL, &err) != DECLINED
2859            && err) {
2860        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
2861    }
2862
2863    /* are the two resources handled by the same repository? */
2864    if (resource->hooks != resnew->hooks) {
2865        /* ### this message exposes some backend config, but screw it... */
2866        return dav_error_response(r, HTTP_BAD_GATEWAY,
2867                                  "Destination URI is handled by a "
2868                                  "different repository than the source URI. "
2869                                  "MOVE or COPY between repositories is "
2870                                  "not possible.");
2871    }
2872
2873    /* get and parse the overwrite header value */
2874    if ((overwrite = dav_get_overwrite(r)) < 0) {
2875        /* dav_get_overwrite() supplies additional information for the
2876         * default message. */
2877        return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
2878    }
2879
2880    /* quick failure test: if dest exists and overwrite is false. */
2881    if (resnew->exists && !overwrite) {
2882        /* Supply some text for the error response body. */
2883        return dav_error_response(r, HTTP_PRECONDITION_FAILED,
2884                                  "Destination is not empty and "
2885                                  "Overwrite is not \"T\"");
2886    }
2887
2888    /* are the source and destination the same? */
2889    if ((*resource->hooks->is_same_resource)(resource, resnew)) {
2890        /* Supply some text for the error response body. */
2891        return dav_error_response(r, HTTP_FORBIDDEN,
2892                                  "Source and Destination URIs are the same.");
2893
2894    }
2895
2896    is_dir = resource->collection;
2897
2898    /* get and parse the Depth header value. "0" and "infinity" are legal. */ view japanese
2899    if ((depth = dav_get_depth(r, DAV_INFINITY)) < 0) {
2900        /* dav_get_depth() supplies additional information for the
2901         * default message. */
2902        return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
2903    }
2904    if (depth == 1) {
2905        /* This supplies additional information for the default message. */
2906        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00593)
2907                      "Depth must be \"0\" or \"infinity\" for COPY or MOVE.");
2908        return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
2909    }
2910    if (is_move && is_dir && depth != DAV_INFINITY) {
2911        /* This supplies additional information for the default message. */
2912        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00594)
2913                      "Depth must be \"infinity\" when moving a collection.");
2914        return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
2915    }
2916
2917    /*
2918     * Check If-Headers and existing locks for each resource in the source.
2919     * We will return a 424 response with a DAV:multistatus body.
2920     * The multistatus responses will contain the information about any
2921     * resource that fails the validation.
2922     *
2923     * We check the parent resource, too, if this is a MOVE. Moving the
2924     * resource effectively removes it from the parent collection, so we
2925     * must ensure that we have met the appropriate conditions.
2926     *
2927     * If a problem occurs with the Request-URI itself, then a plain error
2928     * (rather than a multistatus) will be returned.
2929     */ view japanese
2930    if ((err = dav_validate_request(r, resource, depth, NULL,
2931                                    &multi_response,
2932                                    (is_move ? DAV_VALIDATE_PARENT
2933                                             : DAV_VALIDATE_RESOURCE
2934                                               | DAV_VALIDATE_NO_MODIFY)
2935                                    | DAV_VALIDATE_USE_424,
2936                                    NULL)) != NULL) {
2937        err = dav_push_error(r->pool, err->status, 0,
2938                             apr_psprintf(r->pool,
2939                                          "Could not %s %s due to a failed "
2940                                          "precondition on the source "
2941                                          "(e.g. locks).",
2942                                          is_move ? "MOVE" : "COPY",
2943                                          ap_escape_html(r->pool, r->uri)),
2944                             err);
2945        return dav_handle_err(r, err, multi_response);
2946    }
2947
2948    /*
2949     * Check If-Headers and existing locks for destination. Note that we
2950     * use depth==infinity since the target (hierarchy) will be deleted
2951     * before the move/copy is completed.
2952     *
2953     * Note that we are overwriting the target, which implies a DELETE, so
2954     * we are subject to the error/response rules as a DELETE. Namely, we
2955     * will return a 424 error if any of the validations fail.
2956     * (see dav_method_delete() for more information)
2957     */
2958    if ((err = dav_validate_request(lookup.rnew, resnew, DAV_INFINITY, NULL,
2959                                    &multi_response,
2960                                    DAV_VALIDATE_PARENT
2961                                    | DAV_VALIDATE_USE_424, NULL)) != NULL) {
2962        err = dav_push_error(r->pool, err->status, 0,
2963                             apr_psprintf(r->pool,
2964                                          "Could not MOVE/COPY %s due to a "
2965                                          "failed precondition on the "
2966                                          "destination (e.g. locks).",
2967                                          ap_escape_html(r->pool, r->uri)),
2968                             err);
2969        return dav_handle_err(r, err, multi_response);
2970    }
2971
2972    if (is_dir
2973        && depth == DAV_INFINITY
2974        && (*resource->hooks->is_parent_resource)(resource, resnew)) {
2975        /* Supply some text for the error response body. */
2976        return dav_error_response(r, HTTP_FORBIDDEN,
2977                                  "Source collection contains the "
2978                                  "Destination.");
2979
2980    }
2981    if (is_dir
2982        && (*resnew->hooks->is_parent_resource)(resnew, resource)) {
2983        /* The destination must exist (since it contains the source), and
2984         * a condition above implies Overwrite==T. Obviously, we cannot
2985         * delete the Destination before the MOVE/COPY, as that would
2986         * delete the Source.
2987         */ view japanese
2988
2989        /* Supply some text for the error response body. */ view japanese
2990        return dav_error_response(r, HTTP_FORBIDDEN,
2991                                  "Destination collection contains the Source "
2992                                  "and Overwrite has been specified.");
2993    }
2994
2995    /* ### for now, we don't need anything in the body */ view japanese
2996    if ((result = ap_discard_request_body(r)) != OK) {
2997        return result;
2998    }
2999
3000    if ((err = dav_open_lockdb(r, 0, &lockdb)) != NULL) {
3001        /* ### add a higher-level description? */
3002        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
3003    }
3004
3005    /* remove any locks from the old resources */ view japanese
3006    /*
3007     * ### this is Yet Another Traversal. if we do a rename(), then we
3008     * ### really don't have to do this in some cases since the inode
3009     * ### values will remain constant across the move. but we can't
3010     * ### know that fact from outside the provider :-(
3011     *
3012     * ### note that we now have a problem atomicity in the move/copy
3013     * ### since a failure after this would have removed locks (technically,
3014     * ### this is okay to do, but really...)
3015     */ view japanese
3016    if (is_move && lockdb != NULL) {
3017        /* ### this is wrong! it blasts direct locks on parent resources */ view japanese
3018        /* ### pass lockdb! */ view japanese
3019        (void)dav_unlock(r, resource, NULL);
3020    }
3021
3022    /* if this is a move, then the source parent collection will be modified */ view japanese
3023    if (is_move) {
3024        if ((err = dav_auto_checkout(r, resource, 1 /* parent_only */,
3025                                     &src_av_info)) != NULL) {
3026            if (lockdb != NULL)
3027                (*lockdb->hooks->close_lockdb)(lockdb);
3028
3029            /* ### add a higher-level description? */
3030            return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
3031        }
3032    }
3033
3034    /*
3035     * Remember the initial state of the destination, so the lock system
3036     * can be notified as to how it changed.
3037     */ view japanese
3038    resnew_state = dav_get_resource_state(lookup.rnew, resnew);
3039
3040    /* In a MOVE operation, the destination is replaced by the source.
3041     * In a COPY operation, if the destination exists, is under version
3042     * control, and is the same resource type as the source,
3043     * then it should not be replaced, but modified to be a copy of
3044     * the source.
3045     */ view japanese
3046    if (!resnew->exists)
3047        replace_dest = 0;
3048    else if (is_move || !resource->versioned)
3049        replace_dest = 1;
3050    else if (resource->type != resnew->type)
3051        replace_dest = 1;
3052    else if ((resource->collection == 0) != (resnew->collection == 0))
3053        replace_dest = 1;
3054    else
3055        replace_dest = 0;
3056
3057    /* If the destination must be created or replaced,
3058     * make sure the parent collection is writable
3059     */ view japanese
3060    if (!resnew->exists || replace_dest) {
3061        if ((err = dav_auto_checkout(r, resnew, 1 /*parent_only*/, view japanese
3062                                     &dst_av_info)) != NULL) {
3063            /* could not make destination writable:
3064             * if move, restore state of source parent
3065             */ view japanese
3066            if (is_move) {
3067                (void)dav_auto_checkin(r, NULL, 1 /* undo */, view japanese
3068                                       0 /*unlock*/, &src_av_info);
3069            }
3070
3071            if (lockdb != NULL)
3072                (*lockdb->hooks->close_lockdb)(lockdb);
3073
3074            /* ### add a higher-level description? */
3075            return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
3076        }
3077    }
3078
3079    /* If source and destination parents are the same, then
3080     * use the same resource object, so status updates to one are reflected
3081     * in the other, when doing auto-versioning. Otherwise,
3082     * we may try to checkin the parent twice.
3083     */ view japanese
3084    if (src_av_info.parent_resource != NULL
3085        && dst_av_info.parent_resource != NULL
3086        && (*src_av_info.parent_resource->hooks->is_same_resource)
3087            (src_av_info.parent_resource, dst_av_info.parent_resource)) {
3088
3089        dst_av_info.parent_resource = src_av_info.parent_resource;
3090    }
3091
3092    /* If destination is being replaced, remove it first
3093     * (we know Ovewrite must be TRUE). Then try to copy/move the resource.
3094     */ view japanese
3095    if (replace_dest)
3096        err = (*resnew->hooks->remove_resource)(resnew, &multi_response);
3097
3098    if (err == NULL) {
3099        if (is_move)
3100            err = (*resource->hooks->move_resource)(resource, resnew,
3101                                                    &multi_response);
3102        else
3103            err = (*resource->hooks->copy_resource)(resource, resnew, depth,
3104                                                    &multi_response);
3105    }
3106
3107    /* perform any auto-versioning cleanup */ view japanese
3108    err2 = dav_auto_checkin(r, NULL, err != NULL /* undo if error */,
3109                            0 /*unlock*/, &dst_av_info);
3110
3111    if (is_move) {
3112        err3 = dav_auto_checkin(r, NULL, err != NULL /* undo if error */,
3113                                0 /*unlock*/, &src_av_info);
3114    }
3115    else
3116        err3 = NULL;
3117
3118    /* check for error from remove/copy/move operations */ view japanese
3119    if (err != NULL) {
3120        if (lockdb != NULL)
3121            (*lockdb->hooks->close_lockdb)(lockdb);
3122
3123        err = dav_push_error(r->pool, err->status, 0,
3124                             apr_psprintf(r->pool,
3125                                          "Could not MOVE/COPY %s.",
3126                                          ap_escape_html(r->pool, r->uri)),
3127                             err);
3128        return dav_handle_err(r, err, multi_response);
3129    }
3130
3131    /* check for errors from auto-versioning */ view japanese
3132    if (err2 != NULL) {
3133        /* just log a warning */
3134        err = dav_push_error(r->pool, err2->status, 0,
3135                             "The MOVE/COPY was successful, but there was a "
3136                             "problem automatically checking in the "
3137                             "source parent collection.",
3138                             err2);
3139        dav_log_err(r, err, APLOG_WARNING);
3140    }
3141    if (err3 != NULL) {
3142        /* just log a warning */
3143        err = dav_push_error(r->pool, err3->status, 0,
3144                             "The MOVE/COPY was successful, but there was a "
3145                             "problem automatically checking in the "
3146                             "destination or its parent collection.",
3147                             err3);
3148        dav_log_err(r, err, APLOG_WARNING);
3149    }
3150
3151    /* propagate any indirect locks at the target */ view japanese
3152    if (lockdb != NULL) {
3153
3154        /* notify lock system that we have created/replaced a resource */
3155        err = dav_notify_created(r, lockdb, resnew, resnew_state, depth);
3156
3157        (*lockdb->hooks->close_lockdb)(lockdb);
3158
3159        if (err != NULL) {
3160            /* The move/copy was successful, but the locking failed. */ view japanese
3161            err = dav_push_error(r->pool, err->status, 0,
3162                                 "The MOVE/COPY was successful, but there "
3163                                 "was a problem updating the lock "
3164                                 "information.",
3165                                 err);
3166            return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
3167        }
3168    }
3169
3170    /* return an appropriate response (HTTP_CREATED or HTTP_NO_CONTENT) */ view japanese
3171    return dav_created(r, lookup.rnew->unparsed_uri, "Destination",
3172                       resnew_state == DAV_RESOURCE_EXISTS);
3173}
3174
3175/* dav_method_lock:  Handler to implement the DAV LOCK method
3176 *    Returns appropriate HTTP_* response.
3177 */ view japanese
3178static int dav_method_lock(request_rec *r)
3179{
3180    dav_error *err;
3181    dav_resource *resource;
3182    dav_resource *parent;
3183    const dav_hooks_locks *locks_hooks;
3184    int result;
3185    int depth;
3186    int new_lock_request = 0;
3187    apr_xml_doc *doc;
3188    dav_lock *lock;
3189    dav_response *multi_response = NULL;
3190    dav_lockdb *lockdb;
3191    int resource_state;
3192
3193    /* If no locks provider, decline the request */
3194    locks_hooks = DAV_GET_HOOKS_LOCKS(r);
3195    if (locks_hooks == NULL)
3196        return DECLINED;
3197
3198    if ((result = ap_xml_parse_input(r, &doc)) != OK)
3199        return result;
3200
3201    depth = dav_get_depth(r, DAV_INFINITY);
3202    if (depth != 0 && depth != DAV_INFINITY) {
3203        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00595)
3204                      "Depth must be 0 or \"infinity\" for LOCK.");
3205        return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
3206    }
3207
3208    /* Ask repository module to resolve the resource */
3209    err = dav_get_resource(r, 0 /* label_allowed */, 0 /* use_checked_in */,
3210                           &resource);
3211    if (err != NULL)
3212        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
3213
3214    /* check for any method preconditions */
3215    if (dav_run_method_precondition(r, resource, NULL, doc, &err) != DECLINED
3216            && err) {
3217        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
3218    }
3219
3220    /* Check if parent collection exists */ view japanese
3221    if ((err = resource->hooks->get_parent_resource(resource, &parent)) != NULL) {
3222        /* ### add a higher-level description? */
3223        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
3224    }
3225    if (parent && (!parent->exists || parent->collection != 1)) {
3226        err = dav_new_error(r->pool, HTTP_CONFLICT, 0, 0,
3227                           apr_psprintf(r->pool,
3228                                        "The parent resource of %s does not "
3229                                        "exist or is not a collection.",
3230                                        ap_escape_html(r->pool, r->uri)));
3231        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
3232    }
3233
3234    /*
3235     * Open writable. Unless an error occurs, we'll be
3236     * writing into the database.
3237     */ view japanese
3238    if ((err = (*locks_hooks->open_lockdb)(r, 0, 0, &lockdb)) != NULL) {
3239        /* ### add a higher-level description? */
3240        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
3241    }
3242
3243    if (doc != NULL) {
3244        if ((err = dav_lock_parse_lockinfo(r, resource, lockdb, doc,
3245                                               &lock)) != NULL) {
3246            /* ### add a higher-level description to err? */
3247            goto error;
3248        }
3249        new_lock_request = 1;
3250
3251        lock->auth_user = apr_pstrdup(r->pool, r->user);
3252    }
3253
3254    resource_state = dav_get_resource_state(r, resource);
3255
3256    /*
3257     * Check If-Headers and existing locks.
3258     *
3259     * If this will create a locknull resource, then the LOCK will affect
3260     * the parent collection (much like a PUT/MKCOL). For that case, we must
3261     * validate the parent resource's conditions.
3262     */ view japanese
3263    if ((err = dav_validate_request(r, resource, depth, NULL, &multi_response,
3264                                    (resource_state == DAV_RESOURCE_NULL
3265                                     ? DAV_VALIDATE_PARENT
3266                                     : DAV_VALIDATE_RESOURCE)
3267                                    | (new_lock_request ? lock->scope : 0)
3268                                    | DAV_VALIDATE_ADD_LD,
3269                                    lockdb)) != OK) {
3270        err = dav_push_error(r->pool, err->status, 0,
3271                             apr_psprintf(r->pool,
3272                                          "Could not LOCK %s due to a failed "
3273                                          "precondition (e.g. other locks).",
3274                                          ap_escape_html(r->pool, r->uri)),
3275                             err);
3276        goto error;
3277    }
3278
3279    if (new_lock_request == 0) {
3280        dav_locktoken_list *ltl;
3281
3282        /*
3283         * Refresh request
3284         * ### Assumption:  We can renew multiple locks on the same resource
3285         * ### at once. First harvest all the positive lock-tokens given in
3286         * ### the If header. Then modify the lock entries for this resource
3287         * ### with the new Timeout val.
3288         */ view japanese
3289
3290        if ((err = dav_get_locktoken_list(r, &ltl)) != NULL) {
3291            err = dav_push_error(r->pool, err->status, 0,
3292                                 apr_psprintf(r->pool,
3293                                              "The lock refresh for %s failed "
3294                                              "because no lock tokens were "
3295                                              "specified in an \"If:\" "
3296                                              "header.",
3297                                              ap_escape_html(r->pool, r->uri)),
3298                                 err);
3299            goto error;
3300        }
3301
3302        if ((err = (*locks_hooks->refresh_locks)(lockdb, resource, ltl,
3303                                                 dav_get_timeout(r),
3304                                                 &lock)) != NULL) {
3305            /* ### add a higher-level description to err? */
3306            goto error;
3307        }
3308    } else {
3309        /* New lock request */ view japanese
3310        char *locktoken_txt;
3311        dav_dir_conf *conf;
3312
3313        conf = (dav_dir_conf *)ap_get_module_config(r->per_dir_config,
3314                                                    &dav_module);
3315
3316        /* apply lower bound (if any) from DAVMinTimeout directive */ view japanese
3317        if (lock->timeout != DAV_TIMEOUT_INFINITE
3318            && lock->timeout < time(NULL) + conf->locktimeout)
3319            lock->timeout = time(NULL) + conf->locktimeout;
3320
3321        err = dav_add_lock(r, resource, lockdb, lock, &multi_response);
3322        if (err != NULL) {
3323            /* ### add a higher-level description to err? */ view japanese
3324            goto error;
3325        }
3326
3327        locktoken_txt = apr_pstrcat(r->pool, "<",
3328                                    (*locks_hooks->format_locktoken)(r->pool,
3329                                        lock->locktoken),
3330                                    ">", NULL);
3331
3332        apr_table_setn(r->headers_out, "Lock-Token", locktoken_txt);
3333    }
3334
3335    (*locks_hooks->close_lockdb)(lockdb);
3336
3337    r->status = HTTP_OK;
3338    ap_set_content_type_ex(r, DAV_XML_CONTENT_TYPE, 1);
3339
3340    ap_rputs(DAV_XML_HEADER DEBUG_CR "<D:prop xmlns:D=\"DAV:\">" DEBUG_CR, r);
3341    if (lock == NULL)
3342        ap_rputs("<D:lockdiscovery/>" DEBUG_CR, r);
3343    else {
3344        ap_rprintf(r,
3345                   "<D:lockdiscovery>" DEBUG_CR
3346                   "%s" DEBUG_CR
3347                   "</D:lockdiscovery>" DEBUG_CR,
3348                   dav_lock_get_activelock(r, lock, NULL));
3349    }
3350    ap_rputs("</D:prop>", r);
3351
3352    /* the response has been sent. */ view japanese
3353    return DONE;
3354
3355  error:
3356    (*locks_hooks->close_lockdb)(lockdb);
3357    return dav_handle_err(r, err, multi_response);
3358}
3359
3360/* dav_method_unlock:  Handler to implement the DAV UNLOCK method
3361 *    Returns appropriate HTTP_* response.
3362 */ view japanese
3363static int dav_method_unlock(request_rec *r)
3364{
3365    dav_error *err;
3366    dav_resource *resource;
3367    const dav_hooks_locks *locks_hooks;
3368    int result;
3369    const char *const_locktoken_txt;
3370    char *locktoken_txt;
3371    dav_locktoken *locktoken = NULL;
3372    int resource_state;
3373    dav_response *multi_response;
3374
3375    /* If no locks provider, decline the request */ view japanese
3376    locks_hooks = DAV_GET_HOOKS_LOCKS(r);
3377    if (locks_hooks == NULL)
3378        return DECLINED;
3379
3380    if ((const_locktoken_txt = apr_table_get(r->headers_in,
3381                                             "Lock-Token")) == NULL) {
3382        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00596)
3383                      "Unlock failed (%s):  "
3384                      "No Lock-Token specified in header", r->filename);
3385        return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
3386    }
3387
3388    locktoken_txt = apr_pstrdup(r->pool, const_locktoken_txt);
3389    if (locktoken_txt[0] != '<') {
3390        /* ### should provide more specifics... */
3391        return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
3392    }
3393    locktoken_txt++;
3394
3395    if (locktoken_txt[strlen(locktoken_txt) - 1] != '>') {
3396        /* ### should provide more specifics... */ view japanese
3397        return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
3398    }
3399    locktoken_txt[strlen(locktoken_txt) - 1] = '\0';
3400
3401    if ((err = (*locks_hooks->parse_locktoken)(r->pool, locktoken_txt,
3402                                               &locktoken)) != NULL) {
3403        err = dav_push_error(r->pool, HTTP_BAD_REQUEST, 0,
3404                             apr_psprintf(r->pool,
3405                                          "The UNLOCK on %s failed -- an "
3406                                          "invalid lock token was specified "
3407                                          "in the \"If:\" header.",
3408                                          ap_escape_html(r->pool, r->uri)),
3409                             err);
3410        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
3411    }
3412
3413    /* Ask repository module to resolve the resource */
3414    err = dav_get_resource(r, 0 /* label_allowed */, 0 /* use_checked_in */,
3415                           &resource);
3416    if (err != NULL)
3417        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
3418
3419    /* check for any method preconditions */
3420    if (dav_run_method_precondition(r, resource, NULL, NULL, &err) != DECLINED
3421            && err) {
3422        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
3423    }
3424
3425    resource_state = dav_get_resource_state(r, resource);
3426
3427    /*
3428     * Check If-Headers and existing locks.
3429     *
3430     * Note: depth == 0 normally requires no multistatus response. However,
3431     * if we pass DAV_VALIDATE_PARENT, then we could get an error on a URI
3432     * other than the Request-URI, thereby requiring a multistatus.
3433     *
3434     * If the resource is a locknull resource, then the UNLOCK will affect
3435     * the parent collection (much like a delete). For that case, we must
3436     * validate the parent resource's conditions.
3437     */ view japanese
3438    if ((err = dav_validate_request(r, resource, 0, locktoken,
3439                                    &multi_response,
3440                                    resource_state == DAV_RESOURCE_LOCK_NULL
3441                                    ? DAV_VALIDATE_PARENT
3442                                    : DAV_VALIDATE_RESOURCE, NULL)) != NULL) {
3443        /* ### add a higher-level description? */ view japanese
3444        return dav_handle_err(r, err, multi_response);
3445    }
3446
3447    /* ### RFC 2518 s. 8.11: If this resource is locked by locktoken,
3448     *     _all_ resources locked by locktoken are released.  It does not say
3449     *     resource has to be the root of an infinite lock.  Thus, an UNLOCK
3450     *     on any part of an infinite lock will remove the lock on all resources.
3451     *
3452     *     For us, if r->filename represents an indirect lock (part of an infinity lock),
3453     *     we must actually perform an UNLOCK on the direct lock for this resource.
3454     */ view japanese
3455    if ((result = dav_unlock(r, resource, locktoken)) != OK) {
3456        return result;
3457    }
3458
3459    return HTTP_NO_CONTENT;
3460}
3461
3462static int dav_method_vsn_control(request_rec *r)
3463{
3464    dav_resource *resource;
3465    int resource_state;
3466    dav_auto_version_info av_info;
3467    const dav_hooks_locks *locks_hooks = DAV_GET_HOOKS_LOCKS(r);
3468    const dav_hooks_vsn *vsn_hooks = DAV_GET_HOOKS_VSN(r);
3469    dav_error *err;
3470    apr_xml_doc *doc;
3471    const char *target = NULL;
3472    int result;
3473
3474    /* if no versioning provider, decline the request */ view japanese
3475    if (vsn_hooks == NULL)
3476        return DECLINED;
3477
3478    /* ask repository module to resolve the resource */
3479    err = dav_get_resource(r, 0 /* label_allowed */, 0 /* use_checked_in */,
3480                           &resource);
3481    if (err != NULL)
3482        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
3483
3484    /* parse the request body (may be a version-control element) */ view japanese
3485    if ((result = ap_xml_parse_input(r, &doc)) != OK) {
3486        return result;
3487    }
3488    /* note: doc == NULL if no request body */ view japanese
3489
3490    /* check for any method preconditions */
3491    if (dav_run_method_precondition(r, resource, NULL, doc, &err) != DECLINED
3492            && err) {
3493        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
3494    }
3495
3496    /* remember the pre-creation resource state */ view japanese
3497    resource_state = dav_get_resource_state(r, resource);
3498
3499    if (doc != NULL) {
3500        const apr_xml_elem *child;
3501        apr_size_t tsize;
3502
3503        if (!dav_validate_root(doc, "version-control")) {
3504            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00597)
3505                          "The request body does not contain "
3506                          "a \"version-control\" element.");
3507            return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
3508        }
3509
3510        /* get the version URI */ view japanese
3511        if ((child = dav_find_child(doc->root, "version")) == NULL) {
3512            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00598)
3513                          "The \"version-control\" element does not contain "
3514                          "a \"version\" element.");
3515            return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
3516        }
3517
3518        if ((child = dav_find_child(child, "href")) == NULL) {
3519            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00599)
3520                          "The \"version\" element does not contain "
3521                          "an \"href\" element.");
3522            return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
3523        }
3524
3525        /* get version URI */ view japanese
3526        apr_xml_to_text(r->pool, child, APR_XML_X2T_INNER, NULL, NULL,
3527                        &target, &tsize);
3528        if (tsize == 0) {
3529            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00600)
3530                          "An \"href\" element does not contain a URI.");
3531            return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
3532        }
3533    }
3534
3535    /* Check request preconditions */
3536
3537    /* ### need a general mechanism for reporting precondition violations
3538     * ### (should be returning XML document for 403/409 responses)
3539     */
3540
3541    /* if not versioning existing resource, must specify version to select */ view japanese
3542    if (!resource->exists && target == NULL) {
3543        err = dav_new_error(r->pool, HTTP_CONFLICT, 0, 0,
3544                            "<DAV:initial-version-required/>");
3545        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
3546    }
3547    else if (resource->exists) {
3548        /* cannot add resource to existing version history */ view japanese
3549        if (target != NULL) {
3550            err = dav_new_error(r->pool, HTTP_CONFLICT, 0, 0,
3551                                "<DAV:cannot-add-to-existing-history/>");
3552            return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
3553        }
3554
3555        /* resource must be unversioned and versionable, or version selector */ view japanese
3556        if (resource->type != DAV_RESOURCE_TYPE_REGULAR
3557            || (!resource->versioned && !(vsn_hooks->versionable)(resource))) {
3558            err = dav_new_error(r->pool, HTTP_CONFLICT, 0, 0,
3559                                "<DAV:must-be-versionable/>");
3560            return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
3561        }
3562
3563        /* the DeltaV spec says if resource is a version selector,
3564         * then VERSION-CONTROL is a no-op
3565         */ view japanese
3566        if (resource->versioned) {
3567            /* set the Cache-Control header, per the spec */
3568            apr_table_setn(r->headers_out, "Cache-Control", "no-cache");
3569
3570            /* no body */
3571            ap_set_content_length(r, 0);
3572
3573            return DONE;
3574        }
3575    }
3576
3577    /* Check If-Headers and existing locks */ view japanese
3578    /* Note: depth == 0. Implies no need for a multistatus response. */ view japanese
3579    if ((err = dav_validate_request(r, resource, 0, NULL, NULL,
3580                                    resource_state == DAV_RESOURCE_NULL ?
3581                                    DAV_VALIDATE_PARENT :
3582                                    DAV_VALIDATE_RESOURCE, NULL)) != NULL) {
3583        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
3584    }
3585
3586    /* if in versioned collection, make sure parent is checked out */ view japanese
3587    if ((err = dav_auto_checkout(r, resource, 1 /* parent_only */,
3588                                 &av_info)) != NULL) {
3589        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
3590    }
3591
3592    /* attempt to version-control the resource */ view japanese
3593    if ((err = (*vsn_hooks->vsn_control)(resource, target)) != NULL) {
3594        dav_auto_checkin(r, resource, 1 /*undo*/, 0 /*unlock*/, &av_info);
3595        err = dav_push_error(r->pool, HTTP_CONFLICT, 0,
3596                             apr_psprintf(r->pool,
3597                                          "Could not VERSION-CONTROL resource %s.",
3598                                          ap_escape_html(r->pool, r->uri)),
3599                             err);
3600        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
3601    }
3602
3603    /* revert writability of parent directory */ view japanese
3604    err = dav_auto_checkin(r, resource, 0 /*undo*/, 0 /*unlock*/, &av_info); view japanese view japanese
3605    if (err != NULL) {
3606        /* just log a warning */
3607        err = dav_push_error(r->pool, err->status, 0,
3608                             "The VERSION-CONTROL was successful, but there "
3609                             "was a problem automatically checking in "
3610                             "the parent collection.",
3611                             err);
3612        dav_log_err(r, err, APLOG_WARNING);
3613    }
3614
3615    /* if the resource is lockable, let lock system know of new resource */ view japanese
3616    if (locks_hooks != NULL
3617        && (*locks_hooks->get_supportedlock)(resource) != NULL) {
3618        dav_lockdb *lockdb;
3619
3620        if ((err = (*locks_hooks->open_lockdb)(r, 0, 0, &lockdb)) != NULL) {
3621            /* The resource creation was successful, but the locking failed. */ view japanese
3622            err = dav_push_error(r->pool, err->status, 0,
3623                                 "The VERSION-CONTROL was successful, but there "
3624                                 "was a problem opening the lock database "
3625                                 "which prevents inheriting locks from the "
3626                                 "parent resources.",
3627                                 err);
3628            return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
3629        }
3630
3631        /* notify lock system that we have created/replaced a resource */ view japanese
3632        err = dav_notify_created(r, lockdb, resource, resource_state, 0);
3633
3634        (*locks_hooks->close_lockdb)(lockdb);
3635
3636        if (err != NULL) {
3637            /* The dir creation was successful, but the locking failed. */ view japanese
3638            err = dav_push_error(r->pool, err->status, 0,
3639                                 "The VERSION-CONTROL was successful, but there "
3640                                 "was a problem updating its lock "
3641                                 "information.",
3642                                 err);
3643            return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
3644        }
3645    }
3646
3647    /* set the Cache-Control header, per the spec */
3648    apr_table_setn(r->headers_out, "Cache-Control", "no-cache");
3649
3650    /* return an appropriate response (HTTP_CREATED) */
3651    return dav_created(r, resource->uri, "Version selector", 0 /*replaced*/);
3652}
3653
3654/* handle the CHECKOUT method */ view japanese
3655static int dav_method_checkout(request_rec *r)
3656{
3657    dav_resource *resource;
3658    dav_resource *working_resource;
3659    const dav_hooks_vsn *vsn_hooks = DAV_GET_HOOKS_VSN(r);
3660    dav_error *err;
3661    int result;
3662    apr_xml_doc *doc;
3663    int apply_to_vsn = 0;
3664    int is_unreserved = 0;
3665    int is_fork_ok = 0;
3666    int create_activity = 0;
3667    apr_array_header_t *activities = NULL;
3668
3669    /* If no versioning provider, decline the request */
3670    if (vsn_hooks == NULL)
3671        return DECLINED;
3672
3673    if ((result = ap_xml_parse_input(r, &doc)) != OK)
3674        return result;
3675
3676    if (doc != NULL) {
3677        const apr_xml_elem *aset;
3678
3679        if (!dav_validate_root(doc, "checkout")) {
3680            /* This supplies additional information for the default msg. */
3681            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00601)
3682                          "The request body, if present, must be a "
3683                          "DAV:checkout element.");
3684            return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
3685        }
3686
3687        if (dav_find_child(doc->root, "apply-to-version") != NULL) {
3688            if (apr_table_get(r->headers_in, "label") != NULL) {
3689                /* ### we want generic 403/409 XML reporting here */ view japanese
3690                /* ### DAV:must-not-have-label-and-apply-to-version */ view japanese
3691                return dav_error_response(r, HTTP_CONFLICT,
3692                                          "DAV:apply-to-version cannot be "
3693                                          "used in conjunction with a "
3694                                          "Label header.");
3695            }
3696            apply_to_vsn = 1;
3697        }
3698
3699        is_unreserved = dav_find_child(doc->root, "unreserved") != NULL;
3700        is_fork_ok = dav_find_child(doc->root, "fork-ok") != NULL;
3701
3702        if ((aset = dav_find_child(doc->root, "activity-set")) != NULL) {
3703            if (dav_find_child(aset, "new") != NULL) {
3704                create_activity = 1;
3705            }
3706            else {
3707                const apr_xml_elem *child = aset->first_child;
3708
3709                activities = apr_array_make(r->pool, 1, sizeof(const char *));
3710
3711                for (; child != NULL; child = child->next) {
3712                    if (child->ns == APR_XML_NS_DAV_ID
3713                        && strcmp(child->name, "href") == 0) {
3714                        const char *href;
3715
3716                        href = dav_xml_get_cdata(child, r->pool,
3717                                                 1 /* strip_white */);
3718                        *(const char **)apr_array_push(activities) = href;
3719                    }
3720                }
3721
3722                if (activities->nelts == 0) {
3723                    /* no href's is a DTD violation:
3724                       <!ELEMENT activity-set (href+ | new)>
3725                    */ view japanese
3726
3727                    /* This supplies additional info for the default msg. */ view japanese
3728                    ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00602)
3729                                  "Within the DAV:activity-set element, the "
3730                                  "DAV:new element must be used, or at least "
3731                                  "one DAV:href must be specified.");
3732                    return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
3733                }
3734            }
3735        }
3736    }
3737
3738    /* Ask repository module to resolve the resource */
3739    err = dav_get_resource(r, 1 /*label_allowed*/, apply_to_vsn, &resource); view japanese
3740    if (err != NULL)
3741        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
3742
3743    /* check for any method preconditions */
3744    if (dav_run_method_precondition(r, resource, NULL, doc, &err) != DECLINED
3745            && err) {
3746        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
3747    }
3748
3749    if (!resource->exists) {
3750        /* Apache will supply a default error for this. */
3751        return HTTP_NOT_FOUND;
3752    }
3753
3754    /* Check the state of the resource: must be a file or collection,
3755     * must be versioned, and must not already be checked out.
3756     */ view japanese
3757    if (resource->type != DAV_RESOURCE_TYPE_REGULAR
3758        && resource->type != DAV_RESOURCE_TYPE_VERSION) {
3759        return dav_error_response(r, HTTP_CONFLICT,
3760                                  "Cannot checkout this type of resource.");
3761    }
3762
3763    if (!resource->versioned) {
3764        return dav_error_response(r, HTTP_CONFLICT,
3765                                  "Cannot checkout unversioned resource.");
3766    }
3767
3768    if (resource->working) {
3769        return dav_error_response(r, HTTP_CONFLICT,
3770                                  "The resource is already checked out to the workspace.");
3771    }
3772
3773    /* ### do lock checks, once behavior is defined */
3774
3775    /* Do the checkout */ view japanese
3776    if ((err = (*vsn_hooks->checkout)(resource, 0 /*auto_checkout*/, view japanese
3777                                      is_unreserved, is_fork_ok,
3778                                      create_activity, activities,
3779                                      &working_resource)) != NULL) {
3780        err = dav_push_error(r->pool, HTTP_CONFLICT, 0,
3781                             apr_psprintf(r->pool,
3782                                          "Could not CHECKOUT resource %s.",
3783                                          ap_escape_html(r->pool, r->uri)),
3784                             err);
3785        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
3786    }
3787
3788    /* set the Cache-Control header, per the spec */
3789    apr_table_setn(r->headers_out, "Cache-Control", "no-cache");
3790
3791    /* if no working resource created, return OK,
3792     * else return CREATED with working resource URL in Location header
3793     */ view japanese
3794    if (working_resource == NULL) {
3795        /* no body */
3796        ap_set_content_length(r, 0);
3797        return DONE;
3798    }
3799
3800    return dav_created(r, working_resource->uri, "Checked-out resource", 0);
3801}
3802
3803/* handle the UNCHECKOUT method */ view japanese
3804static int dav_method_uncheckout(request_rec *r)
3805{
3806    dav_resource *resource;
3807    const dav_hooks_vsn *vsn_hooks = DAV_GET_HOOKS_VSN(r);
3808    dav_error *err;
3809    int result;
3810
3811    /* If no versioning provider, decline the request */
3812    if (vsn_hooks == NULL)
3813        return DECLINED;
3814
3815    if ((result = ap_discard_request_body(r)) != OK) {
3816        return result;
3817    }
3818
3819    /* Ask repository module to resolve the resource */
3820    err = dav_get_resource(r, 0 /* label_allowed */, 0 /* use_checked_in */,
3821                           &resource);
3822    if (err != NULL)
3823        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
3824
3825    /* check for any method preconditions */
3826    if (dav_run_method_precondition(r, resource, NULL, NULL, &err) != DECLINED
3827            && err) {
3828        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
3829    }
3830
3831    if (!resource->exists) {
3832        /* Apache will supply a default error for this. */
3833        return HTTP_NOT_FOUND;
3834    }
3835
3836    /* Check the state of the resource: must be a file or collection,
3837     * must be versioned, and must be checked out.
3838     */
3839    if (resource->type != DAV_RESOURCE_TYPE_REGULAR) {
3840        return dav_error_response(r, HTTP_CONFLICT,
3841                                  "Cannot uncheckout this type of resource.");
3842    }
3843
3844    if (!resource->versioned) {
3845        return dav_error_response(r, HTTP_CONFLICT,
3846                                  "Cannot uncheckout unversioned resource.");
3847    }
3848
3849    if (!resource->working) {
3850        return dav_error_response(r, HTTP_CONFLICT,
3851                                  "The resource is not checked out to the workspace.");
3852    }
3853
3854    /* ### do lock checks, once behavior is defined */
3855
3856    /* Do the uncheckout */ view japanese
3857    if ((err = (*vsn_hooks->uncheckout)(resource)) != NULL) {
3858        err = dav_push_error(r->pool, HTTP_CONFLICT, 0,
3859                             apr_psprintf(r->pool,
3860                                          "Could not UNCHECKOUT resource %s.",
3861                                          ap_escape_html(r->pool, r->uri)),
3862                             err);
3863        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
3864    }
3865
3866    /* no body */
3867    ap_set_content_length(r, 0);
3868
3869    return DONE;
3870}
3871
3872/* handle the CHECKIN method */ view japanese
3873static int dav_method_checkin(request_rec *r)
3874{
3875    dav_resource *resource;
3876    dav_resource *new_version;
3877    const dav_hooks_vsn *vsn_hooks = DAV_GET_HOOKS_VSN(r);
3878    dav_error *err;
3879    int result;
3880    apr_xml_doc *doc;
3881    int keep_checked_out = 0;
3882
3883    /* If no versioning provider, decline the request */
3884    if (vsn_hooks == NULL)
3885        return DECLINED;
3886
3887    if ((result = ap_xml_parse_input(r, &doc)) != OK)
3888        return result;
3889
3890    if (doc != NULL) {
3891        if (!dav_validate_root(doc, "checkin")) {
3892            /* This supplies additional information for the default msg. */
3893            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00603)
3894                          "The request body, if present, must be a "
3895                          "DAV:checkin element.");
3896            return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
3897        }
3898
3899        keep_checked_out = dav_find_child(doc->root, "keep-checked-out") != NULL;
3900    }
3901
3902    /* Ask repository module to resolve the resource */
3903    err = dav_get_resource(r, 0 /* label_allowed */, 0 /* use_checked_in */,
3904                           &resource);
3905    if (err != NULL)
3906        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
3907
3908    /* check for any method preconditions */
3909    if (dav_run_method_precondition(r, resource, NULL, doc, &err) != DECLINED
3910            && err) {
3911        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
3912    }
3913
3914    if (!resource->exists) {
3915        /* Apache will supply a default error for this. */
3916        return HTTP_NOT_FOUND;
3917    }
3918
3919    /* Check the state of the resource: must be a file or collection,
3920     * must be versioned, and must be checked out.
3921     */ view japanese
3922    if (resource->type != DAV_RESOURCE_TYPE_REGULAR) {
3923        return dav_error_response(r, HTTP_CONFLICT,
3924                                  "Cannot checkin this type of resource.");
3925    }
3926
3927    if (!resource->versioned) {
3928        return dav_error_response(r, HTTP_CONFLICT,
3929                                  "Cannot checkin unversioned resource.");
3930    }
3931
3932    if (!resource->working) {
3933        return dav_error_response(r, HTTP_CONFLICT,
3934                                  "The resource is not checked out.");
3935    }
3936
3937    /* ### do lock checks, once behavior is defined */
3938
3939    /* Do the checkin */ view japanese
3940    if ((err = (*vsn_hooks->checkin)(resource, keep_checked_out, &new_version))
3941        != NULL) {
3942        err = dav_push_error(r->pool, HTTP_CONFLICT, 0,
3943                             apr_psprintf(r->pool,
3944                                          "Could not CHECKIN resource %s.",
3945                                          ap_escape_html(r->pool, r->uri)),
3946                             err);
3947        return dav_handle_err(r, err, NULL);
3948    }
3949
3950    return dav_created(r, new_version->uri, "Version", 0);
3951}
3952
3953static int dav_method_update(request_rec *r)
3954{
3955    dav_resource *resource;
3956    dav_resource *version = NULL;
3957    const dav_hooks_vsn *vsn_hooks = DAV_GET_HOOKS_VSN(r);
3958    apr_xml_doc *doc;
3959    apr_xml_elem *child;
3960    int is_label = 0;
3961    int depth;
3962    int result;
3963    apr_size_t tsize;
3964    const char *target;
3965    dav_response *multi_response;
3966    dav_error *err;
3967    dav_lookup_result lookup;
3968
3969    /* If no versioning provider, or UPDATE not supported,
3970     * decline the request */ view japanese
3971    if (vsn_hooks == NULL || vsn_hooks->update == NULL)
3972        return DECLINED;
3973
3974    if ((depth = dav_get_depth(r, 0)) < 0) {
3975        /* dav_get_depth() supplies additional information for the
3976         * default message. */
3977        return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
3978    }
3979
3980    /* parse the request body */
3981    if ((result = ap_xml_parse_input(r, &doc)) != OK) {
3982        return result;
3983    }
3984
3985    if (doc == NULL || !dav_validate_root(doc, "update")) {
3986        /* This supplies additional information for the default message. */
3987        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00604)
3988                      "The request body does not contain "
3989                      "an \"update\" element.");
3990        return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
3991    }
3992
3993    /* check for label-name or version element, but not both */ view japanese
3994    if ((child = dav_find_child(doc->root, "label-name")) != NULL)
3995        is_label = 1;
3996    else if ((child = dav_find_child(doc->root, "version")) != NULL) {
3997        /* get the href element */ view japanese
3998        if ((child = dav_find_child(child, "href")) == NULL) {
3999            ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00605)
4000                          "The version element does not contain "
4001                          "an \"href\" element.");
4002            return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
4003        }
4004    }
4005    else {
4006        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, 0, r, APLOGNO(00606)
4007                      "The \"update\" element does not contain "
4008                      "a \"label-name\" or \"version\" element.");
4009        return HTTP_BAD_REQUEST;
4010    }
4011
